item.title: Friday Prayers
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded: Since Nov. 2020, IALFM Masjid is Open for regular (5 daily prayers, inside the masjid) and Friday prayers. The Friday prayer starts at 1:30pm every Friday. Prayers are held in the courtyard or lawn outside masjid with social distancing. IMPORTANT: IF YOU DO NOT FEEL WELL, AND YOU HAVE SYMPTOMS LIKE HIGH GRADE FEVER, COUGH, etc., PLEASE DO NOT COME TO THE MASJID. STAY & PRAY AT YOUR HOME! REQUIREMENTS: All members of the community who plan to attend any of the prayers at the masjid, will be required to: a) Do Wudu at their homes b) Wear face masks c) Bring their own prayer rugs If any member is non-compliant with any of these requirements, he/she shall be asked to leave the Masjid’s property immediately! We really appreciate your utmost cooperation in this regard. We pray to Allah for the safety of our congregation & fellow human beings – Aameen! Let us know via email if you have any questions/concerns. IALFM Board of Directors

item.title: Sample Page
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:

Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)

...or something like this:

The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.

As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

Our website address is: https://www.ialfm.org.

What personal data we collect and why we collect it

Comments

When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Contact forms

Cookies

If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Analytics

Who we share your data with

How long we retain your data

If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where we send your data

Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

Your contact information

Additional information

How we protect your data

What data breach procedures we have in place

What third parties we receive data from

What automated decision making and/or profiling we do with user data

Industry regulatory disclosure requirements

item.title: Annual Community Picnic | Sun Apr 28, 2019
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Masjid is organizing Annual Community Picnic on Sunday April 28, 2019.

The event will kick off at 11am & will end at 3pm.

You can buy tickets online in advance of the event to avoid lines & ensure a quick entry with bands (for food & beverages) and 'Picnic Bucks' (needed for games & activities).

Click on the 'Buy Tickets' button above to buy tickets & picnic bucks online using your Credit Card or eCheck/ACH.

ONLY if you buy in advance, you'll receive a significant discount on Picnic Bucks, i.e. 12 Bucks for $10 and 25 Bucks for $20.

Picnic Bucks will be $1 each on the day of the picnic.

We highly recommend you to buy the $20 Picnic Bucks package.

All money raised thru ticket sales at the picnic will go towards the operational & construction costs of our Masjid!

Our sincere thanks to you for being a part of this event and activities.

Games & Activities This Year

Yes - We really want EVERYONE to come & have fun - Adults, Kids & especially our Youth! It should not be boring for anyone.

To ensure that this year we are bringing back the popular games & activities from last year, like ...

Assorted Chaat & Refreshments Stalls
Bouncy Houses
Pony Rides & Face Painting along with a few other fun games & activities that are new this year, like ...

IALFM Sunday School Activities

Our Sunday School is also organizing different activities which include ...

Bike-a-Thon, Cake Walk, Bake Sale, Carnival Games, Gift Basket Auction,
Fruit Kabobs, Chaat Stalls & Tug of War

Please come and attend the fabulous picnic event planned for you and your family!

We hope to see all of you there!

Let us know if you will have any questions by dropping us an email at picnic@ialfm.org

item.title: SS Staff Registration (backup)
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Sunday School Staff Registration (2023-24)

Our Sunday School is run solely by volunteers. We have different positions available depending on your educational background, skills, experience & availability. If you are interested in volunteering for our Sunday School, please complete the registration form below after reading the following instructions.

Important – Please Read Before Submitting Volunteer Form...

  1. All interested individuals should register by completing & submitting this form.
  2. All classes will be offered in-person, 1st semester (Aug thru Dec 2023) and 2nd semester (Jan thru May 2024). Classes start on Aug 20th, 2023.
  3. Sunday school timings will be from 10:30am to 01:45pm, 2 classes of 75 mins each with a 30 mins break in between, and 15 mins for Dhuhr Salah.
  4. Final placement for each position will be assigned by the Sunday School Committee.
  5. We will have a 'Staff Orientation' on Aug 13th, 2023. Details will be shared later, iA
  6. Have questions? Send us an e-mail at sundayschool@ialfm.org

Sign-up here: https://forms.office.com/r/VcFcwPk4nB

item.title: Interfaith Summer Camp
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Interfaith Summer Camp is an annual event organized by IALFM Youth Committee in collaboration with other faith based institutions in our neighborhood which includes churches, temples, synagogues & other congregations, such as Creekwood Christian Church, Flower Mound Hindu Temple, Baha’is of Flower Mound & Congregation Kol Ami.

The camp is organized for children & adults (age 3 & above) with activities planned at different locations, mainly religious institutions of participating organizations. Over the course of 5 or more days, congregations from participating faith-based organizations attend events at those locations focused on the main theme of the Interfaith Summer Camp.

Interfaith Summer Camp provides a wonderful opportunity to celebrate the diversity in our community!

The theme for 2017 was ‘Seek and You Shall Find‘. Each night, the hosting faith community helps participants better understand what faithful followers seek from their religious practices. This unique experience provides all participants an opportunity to learn, share, befriend & build a greater understanding of their friends & peers from multiple faith & religious backgrounds.

Each hosting faith community is responsible for coming up with a program for the day when they host camp participants at their location. The daily program generally consists of a potluck dinner, some games and learning exercises for children & adults and usually wraps up with a lecture or other suitable activity to facilitate better understanding of religious beliefs of hosting faith community.

The teachings focus on building relationships and bridges through fun, experiential activities that teach the basic of each faith and celebrate common values. This unique experience is an opportunity to share, learn, befriend, and build a greater understanding between Faith-based traditions.

Last year in 2016, the theme was ‘Honoring the Divine and Honoring Each Other‘. All participants including children, teens and adults gathered for a week of learning about Hinduism, Judaism, Christianity, Islam and Baha’i faiths. Each evening, participants visited a different house of worship where they shared a halal meal, got together into different age groups for informative learning, fun activities, and conversations with participants from other faiths.

The final day of Interfaith Summer Camp is generally a celebration dedicated to having fun together and allowing campers an opportunity to share with their parents what they have learned and created during that week. Parents are also invited to join the camp for the entire day.

Dress Guidelines
As a sign of respect for the different houses of worship that participants visit, it is requested that all volunteers and campers honor the following dress code while at Interfaith Summer Camp.

– Wear closed toe shoes & wear socks
– No shorts, wear jeans, or floor length skirts
– Do not wear any sleeveless t-shirts or tank tops
– Do not wear t-shirts with slogans on them
– Headscarf for girls is recommended

For more info, please visit: Denton South Interfaith‘s Summer Camp page on Facebook

item.title: Education & Youth Director
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Br Haris Qudsi is working as Education & Youth Director at IALFM since October, 2018. In his position, he works directly with the IALFM Board, Education, Sunday School & Youth Committees on the planning of existing and new educational and social programs.

Having spent the last 6 years studying under the tutelage of exceptional scholars such as Mufti Hussain Kamani, Sheikh Abdul Nasir Jangda and Sheikh Yaseen Shaikh, Br Haris Qudsi is currently in the final year of his formal Alimiyyah studies. A former member of the US Army, Br Haris has a unique perspective on growing up as Muslim American, having served as the Vice President of his University Muslim Students Association, as well as a leader for his Masjid Youth Group. Br Haris also served as a chaplain for the MSA of his alma mater, working with college students to help grow them in their faith.

IALFM serves a growing Muslim community with an active base. This is an active center providing services for both genders of all ages. IALFM has different programs focusing on multiple segments of our community and activities that are designed to provide supplemental spiritual, social, educational, and Islamic benefits to the IALFM Muslim community.

There are existing programs, such as Sunday School, Friday Family Night, Ramadan Programs, Sisters Educational Classes, Youth Halaqah, Coffee Talk, Youth Conferences, Dawah & Outreach programs focusing on Inter-Faith harmony & other topics. These activities have mostly been managed on a voluntary basis. In order to maximize the performance and efficiency of these programs, IALFM has created a full-time ‘Education and Youth Director’ position to oversee and manage existing programs as well as to add new programs.

As Education and Youth Director, Br Haris Qudsi is responsible for organizing educational programs for our community based on the Holy Quran and Sunnah of the Prophet (SAW). He works with the IALFM Board and relevant committees (Education, Sunday School, Youth, etc.) to coordinate and plan existing & new programs.

Specific responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  1. Plan and organize a calendar of educational events for all community members (men, women, children & youth)
  2. Friday Sermons (Khutbah), as needed
  3. Educational Services, such as Classes on a regular basis on different topics, Halaqah for Adults, Friday Family Night (Halaqah) & Educational classes for Sisters
  4. Youth activities, such as Interactive Halaqah that engages participants, Experiential learning through sports, games, outings & other fun activities, Counseling, as needed
  5. Advises on Sunday School Curriculum, takes at least one class for higher level students
  6. Use digital channels to engage the community and reinforce learning
  7. Support Outreach Programs (Dawah, Interfaith dialogue)
  8. Help with Masjid Open House, serve as a speaker & representative for interfaith programs
  9. Speak at Fundraising events for IALFM & represent IALFM at such events
  10. Ramadan Programs (daily khatirah, etc.)
  11. Working with the IALFM BOD & Imam to address community problems
  12. Measure event performance and provide reports on effectiveness of organized programs

Plan for Youth & Education

Br Haris recently shared his plan for Youth & Education with the community at a Friday Family Night event on Nov 11 at our masjid. In his presentation, he introduced himself, talked about his role at IALFM, his philosophy & approach (‘The Golden Circle’; What, How & Why), Red, White & Blue Phases, his expectations from our community & took questions from attendees at the end.

Please review his slides: IALFM Plan for Youth & Education

Contact Info and Office Hours for Br Haris Qudsi

You can contact Br Haris Qudsi via email at: ed@ialfm.org

Office Hours: You can also meet him in person at IALFM Masjid premises on Tuesday & Thursday from 6 to 8pm.

item.title: Instructions for 'IALFM Live' Meeting
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Assalam-o-Alaikum - In order to attend the 'IALFM Live' meeting that has been scheduled by IALFM, please follow these instructions:

  1. Install Microsoft Teams app on your computer or mobile device of any type (i.e. Windows, Mac, Apple iOS, Google Android) by going to this website: https://teams.microsoft.com/downloads (installing Teams app on your computer or mobile device is highly recommended for best experience)

    Note: If you are planning to join from a computer (Windows or Mac), you can join the meeting using an internet browser as well, such as Chrome, Edge, Firefox or Safari. Just click on the 'Join on the web instead' button in the browser window, after Step 3. However, on mobile devices, you will have to install Teams app to join this meeting.
  2. After the app installation completes, there is no need for you to sign up for a free account or sign in using any type of account that you may already have
  3. At the time of the meeting or a few minutes before it, i.e. 7pm CT, click on this link: Join Microsoft Teams Meeting
  4. When the Microsoft Teams app comes up (after you clicked on the above link to join the meeting), click on 'Join as a guest' option to join the meeting (again, no need to sign in)
  5. On the next screen, provide your full name (like, Ahmad Khan) and then click on 'Join meeting' button to join the meeting
  6. After this, you'll just end up in the meeting with the organizer and other attendees like you
  7. Happy meeting!

    For Technical Issues: Please send an email to live@ialfm.org

  4. When the Microsoft Teams app comes up (after you clicked on the above link to join the meeting), click on 'Join as a guest' option to join the meeting (again, no need to sign in)
  5. On the next screen, provide your full name (i.e. Shabana Khan) and then click on 'Join meeting' button to join the meeting
  6. After this, you'll just end up in the meeting with the organizer and other attendees like you!
  7. Happy meeting!

item.title: Ramadan 2018
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Asaalam o Alaikum! 

We pray to Allah (swt) for his blessings and mercy during the auspicious month of Ramadan. As per the official announcement of Fiqh Council of North America (link), the first day of Ramadan 1439 is on Wednesday, May 16, 2018, Insha’Allah. First Tarawih prayer will be on Tuesday (May 15) night.

Ramadan Programs

Our masjid incurs $15,000 of additional expenses during Ramadan and we need community support to help pay for these expenses. We are requesting a donation of at least $100 per family to cover these expenses. Please donate generously by clicking on the link below.

Donate for Ramadan Programs

Use Refillable Water Bottles

In an effort to be environmentally responsible, we will not be providing bottled water during Community Iftars, Tarawih & other activities during Ramadan. Community members are strongly encouraged to bring their own labeled and refillable water bottles. Multiple water stations (with disposable cups) will be placed around the masjid property at convenient locations, where reusable water bottles can be filled. We request your cooperation for the success of this initiative!

Community Iftar

Insha Allah, we will have 'Community Iftar' every week on Friday, Saturday & Sunday. Each Iftar will be hosted by 7 families. Interested families can also participate by donating any amount for Iftar on 27th of Ramadan, which would be hosted by IALFM.

For more information & guidelines, please review this document: Community Iftar Guidelines

To signup for an Iftar slot, please see the IFTAR SIGNUP SHEET

*  Click on the link above to open the signup sheet & enter your Full Name & Cell Phone Number to signup for a slot

*  Iftar slot signup is on 'First come, First served' basis

*  Please do not over-write on any slots that are already taken

Bake Sale on Saturdays & Sundays

We will also have Bake Sale on specific Saturdays & Sundays.

To signup for a Bake Sale slot, please see the BAKE SALE SIGNUP SHEET

Tarawih

Our Imam Rasheed Farah & Hafiz Muhammad Thayyil will lead the Tarawih prayers at our masjid.

Ramadan Calendar

See below for a quick glance | Ramadan Calendar (PDF, click on this link to download & print the calendar)

Questions or Comments

Please send Ramadan Committee an email at ramadan@ialfm.org

Ramadan Updates

If you wish to receive regular updates on our Ramadan activities, please signup for our weekly newsletter!

item.title: Sunday School Student Registration - [Backup Page]
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
BACKUP FROM 2025-26 (9/5/25):

Please read the important instructions below before submitting registration form.

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. If you want to register multiple children, please complete & submit this form for each child separately.
2. All students must be registered, both new & returning/continuing students.
3. For Fee info, please see 'Sunday School Overview' page on our website.
4. Payments need to be submitted in-person on 'Registration Day' on 10th August, 2025. Books will be provided on the same day.
5. All returning students will be given priority, if they register by August 3rd, 2025. After that we fallback to our 'first come, first served' policy for all students.
6. Sunday school timing is from 10:30am to 01:45pm, i.e. 2 classes of 75 mins each with a 30 mins break & 15 mins for Dhuhr Salah at the end.
8. If you have any questions, please send us an e-mail at sundayschool@ialfm.org

Student Registration Form: https://forms.office.com/r/tdjSgEbf9B (direct link) or use the form below.

BACKUP OF PREVIOUS YEAR: 2024-25

Please read the important points below before submitting registration form.

NOTE: Starting on August 15th, newly registered kids in Grades 1st thru 4th will be put on 'Waiting List' as we've reached enrollment limit for those grades. Admissions will be offered to such kids if/when space is available in future.

IMPORTANT:
1. If you want to register multiple children, please complete & submit this form for each child separately.
2. All students must be registered, both new and returning/continuing students.
3. All returning students will be given priority, if they register by Sunday August 11th, 2024.
4. Please read all details at: https://www.ialfm.org/ss-overview/, check out new class schedule, school timings remain the same as before. For most levels, there will be no need to buy textbooks.
5. Payments need to be submitted in-person on Registration + Orientation Day on Sunday August 18th, 2024. See Calendar at: https://www.ialfm.org/ss-calendar/
6. If you have any questions, please send us an e-mail at sundayschool@ialfm.org

< OLDER CONTENT BELOW >

Steps to Register Your Child in Sunday School

Sunday School Registration is open depending on the availability of space in different classes!
Register your child using this link: Student Registration Form (the same form is also available below). Admissions will be on the basis of availability of space in classes for students in different grades.

Questions: Let us know via email if you have any questions.

Important Details

  1. This year classes will start on August 20th, 2023, and go through May 2024 (with Ramadan break in March)
    ﻿
  2. Sunday School will start at 10:30am & end at 1:45pm.
    ﻿
  3. There will be two classes (Quran & Religion) of 75 mins each for all levels (Level 1 thru 4), 30 minsbreak, and 15 mins for Dhuhr Salah (snacks are provided to children during break time at no extra charge, students are encouraged to bring their own lunch from home, if needed)
    ﻿
  4. All returning students will be given priority if they register by July 31st, 2023
    ﻿

Any other questions or feedback, you can reach us at: sundayschool@ialfm.org

item.title: Zoom Accounts for IALFM Committees
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
We have two professional Zoom accounts (no time limit for meetings, maximum 100 attendees, meetings can be recorded) available for use by different IALFM Committees for their online meetings, programs and events. IALFM Committee leads or members can send a quick email to IALFM Board with the required info (see basic simple steps below) to book a particular Zoom account for their use. Let us know if we can make it more convenient for you in any way, your feedback is always welcome!

Basic Simple Rules

  1. Check the schedule of Zoom accounts in their respective calendars linked below, to find an available spot for your meeting or event or see if the time slot if available, when you plan to have your meeting/event
  2. To book a Zoom account for use, send an email to IALFM Board with the following details:
    Zoom account number you want to book, brief event details, date, time, single instance or recurring event, end date if it's a recurring event
  3. Send the booking email well in advance, preferably weeks in advance for processing. Minimum notice of 24 to 48 hours is required for processing these requests, otherwise a response is not guaranteed
  4. For approved requests, our response will include login details of a Zoom account which can be used for the event & the event will be placed on Zoom account's calendar
  5. When requests are received less than 24 hours before the event, a committee can use a particular Zoom account, if it's not already booked for another event, otherwise please use personal Zoom accounts for your event

Zoom Account Calendars

IALFM Zoom1

IALFM Zoom2

item.title: Refund and Returns Policy
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
This is a sample page.

Overview

Our refund and returns policy lasts 30 days. If 30 days have passed since your purchase, we can’t offer you a full refund or exchange.

To be eligible for a return, your item must be unused and in the same condition that you received it. It must also be in the original packaging.

Several types of goods are exempt from being returned. Perishable goods such as food, flowers, newspapers or magazines cannot be returned. We also do not accept products that are intimate or sanitary goods, hazardous materials, or flammable liquids or gases.

Additional non-returnable items:

  • Gift cards
  • Downloadable software products
  • Some health and personal care items

To complete your return, we require a receipt or proof of purchase.

Please do not send your purchase back to the manufacturer.

There are certain situations where only partial refunds are granted:

  • Book with obvious signs of use
  • CD, DVD, VHS tape, software, video game, cassette tape, or vinyl record that has been opened.
  • Any item not in its original condition, is damaged or missing parts for reasons not due to our error.
  • Any item that is returned more than 30 days after delivery

Refunds

Once your return is received and inspected, we will send you an email to notify you that we have received your returned item. We will also notify you of the approval or rejection of your refund.

If you are approved, then your refund will be processed, and a credit will automatically be applied to your credit card or original method of payment, within a certain amount of days.

Late or missing refunds

If you haven’t received a refund yet, first check your bank account again.

Then contact your credit card company, it may take some time before your refund is officially posted.

Next contact your bank. There is often some processing time before a refund is posted.

If you’ve done all of this and you still have not received your refund yet, please contact us at {email address}.

Sale items

Only regular priced items may be refunded. Sale items cannot be refunded.

Exchanges

We only replace items if they are defective or damaged. If you need to exchange it for the same item, send us an email at {email address} and send your item to: {physical address}.

Gifts

If the item was marked as a gift when purchased and shipped directly to you, you’ll receive a gift credit for the value of your return. Once the returned item is received, a gift certificate will be mailed to you.

If the item wasn’t marked as a gift when purchased, or the gift giver had the order shipped to themselves to give to you later, we will send a refund to the gift giver and they will find out about your return.

Shipping returns

To return your product, you should mail your product to: {physical address}.

You will be responsible for paying for your own shipping costs for returning your item. Shipping costs are non-refundable. If you receive a refund, the cost of return shipping will be deducted from your refund.

Depending on where you live, the time it may take for your exchanged product to reach you may vary.

If you are returning more expensive items, you may consider using a trackable shipping service or purchasing shipping insurance. We don’t guarantee that we will receive your returned item.

Need help?

Contact us at {email} for questions related to refunds and returns.

item.title: Plan your visit
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded: [section extra="single-post-header clearfix"] [heading size="h2" extra="post-title"]Here is some info to help you plan your visit to IALFM Masjid![/heading] [/section] [section extra="post-content"] [paragraph extra=""][label type="label-danger"]L O C A T I O N[/label] We are located at 3430 Peters Colony Road in Flower Mound, TX 75022 right across Flower Mound High School in the neighborhood of other religious (churches) & educational (schools) organizations. We are also walking distance from Flower Mound Public Library and other amenities, like stores and eateries.[/paragraph] [paragraph extra=""][label type="label-danger"]D I R E C T I O N S[/label] We would definitely recommend using popular websites for driving directions as we are properly mentioned in their maps, such as Google Maps or Bing Maps. We are located in between I-35S East and West which places us at an ideal & accessible location in greater DFW area, within 15 to 20 minutes drive from surrounding suburbs of Irving, Grapevine, Carrollton, Colony, Highland Village & Coppell. Other major US & TX major highways such as I-635, TX-114 & TX-121 also conveniently link us to surrounding areas.[/paragraph] [container extra="row"] [one_half extra="" anim=""] [paragraph extra=""][label type="label-danger"]P A R K I N G[/label] We have sufficient parking available on our own premises and at the time of major events like Friday prayer, Ramadan & Eid prayers, we are able to utilize our neighboring Christ Presbyterian Church's multiple parking lots (not available on Sundays) & huge parking lot of Flower Mound High School (not available during school days & hours). At times we also suggest our community members to use parking lot of Flower Mound Public Library as its at a manageable walking distance (please first drop the elderly, children & women before heading towards library to park there, its at a short 5 minutes walk).[/paragraph] [/one_half] [one_half extra="" anim=""] [paragraph extra=""][label type="label-danger"]W U D U  &  R E S T R O O M S[/label] We have Wudu area (4/5 seats) available outside the masjid near Women's entrance for men. For Women there are multiple restrooms available inside the masjid with Wudu facilities. Men's restroom is also located inside masjid, entrance is at the back side adjacent to Men's Wudu area. Please feel free to ask members of our community for directions, if you need any assistance.[/paragraph] [/one_half] [/container] [/section]

item.title: About Us
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded: Islamic Association of Lewisville-Flower Mound or IALFM is a registered non-profit religious organization. IALFM is a designated 501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization under prevalent IRS laws & is eligible to receive tax deductible donations & contributions (our Federal Tax ID # is 75-2892048). IALFM is an independent member-based organization and operates strictly under the religious guidance of the Islamic Association of North Texas (IANT). IALFM is dedicated to worship, education, and community service in the Flower Mound, Lewisville, Highland Village, Double Oak, Lantana, Lake Dallas, Grapevine, and surrounding areas. We are located 15 minutes north of the Dallas Fort Worth (DFW) airport, next to the Flower Mound High School.

item.title: Board of Directors
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
The day-to-day affairs of IALFM are conducted by the members of Board of Directors (BoD). The IALFM Board has seven elected members from our community and is governed by the IALFM Constitution. Following is a list of all current members of our Board.

Br Masud Rahman - President (elected for 2026-27 term)
Br Harris Khan - Vice President (elected for 2025-26 term)
Sr Rahila Siddiqui - Secretary (elected for 2026-27 term)
Br Jamal Ahmed - Treasurer (elected for 2025-26 term)
Br Babar Bhatti (elected for 2026-27 term)
Br Mahmood Alvi (elected for 2025-26 term)
Br Syed Bukhari (elected for 2025-26 term)

You can contact the IALFM 'Board of Directors' by sending an email at bod@ialfm.org.

Past Directors

Click here to show details about 'Past Directors' ...

Keeping this section for record purposes as it helps during election process and nomination of new candidates for available director positions in IALFM Board of Directors.

Br Masud Rahman (elected for 2024-25 term, completed his term on 12/31/2025 & got re-elected for 2026-27 term, i.e. his 2nd consecutive term)
Br Firoz Vohra (elected for 2024-25 term, completed his term on 12/31/2025)
Br Harris Khan (elected for 2023-24 term, completed his term on 12/31/2024 & got re-elected for 2025-26 term, i.e. his 2nd consecutive term)
Br Razaq Khazi-Syed (elected & served for two continuous terms, 2022-23 & 2024-25, completed his last term on 12/31/2025)
Br Lisanul Haq (elected for 2024-25 term, resigned on 12/31/2024)
Sr Kulsum Pirbhai (elected for 2023-24 term, completed her term on 12/31/2024)
Br Nusrat Atta (elected for 2023-24 term, completed his term on 12/31/2024)
Br Amir Haque (elected for 2022-23 term, completed his term on 12/31/2023)
Br Jamal Ahmed (elected for 2022-23 term, completed his term on 12/31/2023)
Br Mohammad Qasim (elected for 2022-23 term, completed his term on 12/31/2023)
Sr Tooba Mohiuddin (elected for 2021-22 term, completed her term on 12/31/2022)
Br Ali Emran (elected & served for two continuous terms, 2019-20 & 2021-22, substituted for Br Tahir Farrukh after his resignation, completed his last term on 12/31/2022)
Br Gamal Abdel-Hafiz (elected & served for two continuous terms, 2019-20 & 2021-22, completed his last term on 12/31/2022)
Br Kazal Ahmed (elected for 2020-21 term, completed his term on 12/31/2021)
Br Ali Khokhar (elected for 2020-21 term, completed his term on 12/31/2021)

Br Tahir Farrukh (elected for 2021-22 term, resigned on 7/6/2021)

Br Razaq Khazi-Syed (elected for 2018-19 term, completed his term on 12/31/2019)
Sr Samia Babar (elected for 2018-19 term, completed her term on 12/31/2019)
Br Asif Khan (elected for 2018-19 term, completed his term on 12/31/2019)
Br Ahmed Elsaie (elected for 2019-20 term, resigned on 10/5/2019)
Br Mahmood Anwar (substituted for Br Sher Khan after his resignation, resigned on 9/27/2019)
Br Sher Khan (elected for 2019-20 term, resigned on 4/18/2019)
Br Mujtaba Hassan (elected for 2017-18 term, completed his term on 12/31/2018)
Br Amir Haque (elected for 2017-18 term, completed his term on 12/31/2018)
Br Nasir Malik (elected for 2018-19 term, resigned on 11/15/2018)
Br Ali Mehmud (elected for 2018-19 term, resigned on 11/11/2018)

Note: As per IALFM Constitution (Article 6.3.1), a director in IALFM 'Board of Directors' is elected for a term of 2 years & can serve 2 consecutive terms after going thru election process.

item.title: BoD Meetings
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Board of Directors meets every week on Wednesdays (or other week-days, depending on schedule) in the evening at the IALFM Masjid and/or virtually over Zoom. At other times the frequency decreases, especially during Ramadan and holidays period.

Can IALFM Members join BOD Meeting? - Yes!

IALFM members can meet with the BOD at that time to discuss any issue that they want to pursue with the board. BOD can reserve first 15 minutes of a meeting for discussion with IALFM members on a topic of their interest. If interested, please send an email to bod@ialfm.org to request a time slot.

item.title: Committees
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
As a volunteer-run organization IALFM depends heavily on responsible volunteers for its various functions and services. BOD has commissioned different committees to serve the needs of the community. These committees consist of volunteers from our community. Most committees have at least one BOD Representative who may also head the committee, if needed, while other committees are lead by volunteers from our community.

Guidance for Community

Please feel free to directly contact IALFM Committees (listed below) using their email addresses, in case if you have a question for them on related responsibilities & activities.

Want to Volunteer?

See: Sign-up as a Volunteer

Inter-Committees Communication

Members of IALFM Committees should feel free to communicate with each other thru available means, in person or preferably via email (as it helps in keeping a record of contact/conversation). When needed, members can contact IALFM BOD (email) with their questions or concerns.

IALFM Committees

Audit – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee is responsible for ensuring that the annual financial audit of our organization, as required per our constitution, is conducted & report is prepared for the reference of IALFM members. Financial background is highly recommended for members who wish to serve on this committee.

Careers – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee is responsible for organizing and managing the entire hiring process for open employment positions at IALFM, like Youth Advisor, etc.

Constitution Review – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee is responsible for soliciting community feedback regarding the constitution & recommend changes to the Board & IALFM General Body (members) for approval. Legal background is highly recommended to serve on this committee.

Construction – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee oversees the construction project & process for our new masjid building that we are building for our future community use. An engineering degree/background & project management experience is highly desired to serve on this committee.

Education – Contact: email

Education committee is responsible for organizing & producing Islamic educational programs and guidance for the IALFM community and for managing the Quran school and Pillars Academy. The educational programs include khatiras, lectures, classes, workshops, activities, family nights and more. Please note that Sunday School is managed by a separate committee.

Election Commission – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

Election Commission is responsible for managing the entire election process for IALFM Board of Directors or any other similar need where consent or vote of IALFM General Body is required to conduct the business of IALFM organization.

Emergency Relief Fund (ERF) & Donations Disbursement – Contact: email (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee processes applications for financial help from deserving community members & donation disbursement to local charitable organizations, funds are paid from Zakat & Sadaqa collections.

Facility – Contact: email

This committee is responsible for maintenance of the masjid building & its premises. Besides overseeing regular maintenance, it is also responsible for authorization & tracking of its use for approved events organized at our masjid.

Finance – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee is overseen by the treasurer & is responsible for financial matters of our organization including account receivables, account payable, bookkeeping & budgeting. Accounting background is highly desirable to serve on this committee.

Fundraising – Contact: email

This committee oversees the fundraising aspects & events for our organization. The funds raised could be for a specific purpose, like the construction of the new masjid building or for general operational expenses of the organization.

Funeral – Contact: email

This committee manages the funeral services that are provided by IALFM to our community.

Interfaith Outreach & Dawah – Contact: email

This committee helps facilitate interfaith harmony working in collaboration with other local faith based organizations & organizes related events.

Khateeb Scheduling – Contact: email

This committee oversees the scheduling of speakers mainly for Friday prayers, Ramadan & other similar needs during the year.

Membership – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee manages the signup & maintenance of the paid members of our organization. This committee maintains the members list used for election of the Board of Directors & organizes the general body meetings.

Picnic – Contact: email - (not looking for new members at this time)

This committee helps in organizing the 'Annual Community Picnic' in Spring with help from Sunday School, Facility & other committees at IALFM.

Ramadan – Contact: email

This committee helps organize community Iftaar, Taraweeh & other related events during the Islamic month of Ramadan.

Security – Contact: email

This committee is responsible for the physical security of the premises, assets and visitors of our masjid.

Sisters – Contact: email

This committee helps organize Islamic, educational, and charitable events specifically for the sisters in our community.

Sunday School – Contact: email

This committee is solely responsible for the educational aspects & operations of ‘IALFM Sunday School’ for the children in Pre-K thru 8th grades.

Youth – Contact: email

This committee helps organize events for the youth in our community, solicits their feedback & provides their perspective to the following committees: Education, Fundraising & Sunday School.

item.title: Construction Project
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Current Status (as of September 2023)

Alhumdulillah! Our New Masjid building construction project concluded in 2021 with the help & support of our IALFM & greater DFW community.

History

Earlier in April 2021, the construction work was mostly completed, and we also received a permit from the Town of Flower Mound to start using the facility, after a thorough inspection. We also had a formal opening ceremony during Ramadan on 27th Ramadan (May 8, 2021). Since then, we have been using our new building for Friday prayers & other important large events, like Eid & Taraweeh prayers during Ramadan, when we usually have a large number of attendees at our masjid. Later in 2023, we also started utilizing our new building for daily prayers & all other smaller events.

Check out the pictures of our new building at the bottom of this page!

Past Milestones

March 2020: IALFM Board & Construction Committee signed the contract for the third & final phase of our new masjid construction project. The construction work started in April 2020 & continued at a steady pace for the next one year.

August 2018: Phase 1 & Phase 2 were completed. These phases included Foundation of our 15,000 square feet new building and 93 additional parking places in the New Parking Lot on top of existing parking that we have at our masjid. New Parking Lot was inaugurated for public use after approval of related permits from the Town of Flower Mound. Since then, the parking lot has been used regularly by visitors coming to our mosque, especially during Friday prayers & major events like Eid prayers, etc.

Financial Summary

Total amount spent on Phase 1 & Phase 2: $1.2M
Total cost of Final Phase 3: $2.1M
All loans have been paid off, and our masjid is now debt-free, Alhumdulillah!

For more info & questions, please contact IALFM Board at bod@ialfm.org

Construction Site Pictures & Video

Construction Site Updates from the past are available on our website & our Facebook page.

New Building Construction Site Videos are also available on our YouTube channel

item.title: Contact Us
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Physical Address

IALFM is located at 3430 Peters Colony Road in Flower Mound, TX 75022

Phone Number

Our Phone Number is: 972-355-3937 (please leave us a message with your contact information and we will get back to you)

Contact via Email

You can also send us an email at info@ialfm.org

Our Location on Map

item.title: Our Imam
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Imam Rasheed Farah was born in Somalia. By Allah’s grace he became a Hafiz at the tender age of ten. He studied Tafseer, Arabic, Fiqh, Tajweed & Seerah from different scholars. To further his knowledge of Islam, he studied for two years at the American International University, an online university that offers courses in Islamic Studies. He worked as an Assistant Imam for three years at Islamic Society of Arlington, Texas.

He then became Imam of IALFM Masjid, managed thru Islamic Association of Lewisville-Flower Mound, in 2006. Since then he has been serving the community as our Imam where he usually heads daily & Friday prayers. Imam also leads Tarawih prayers in Ramadan and Eid prayers annually at the masjid.

Ask Imam A Question

You can send your questions to Imam Rasheed at imam@ialfm.org

item.title: General Body Meetings
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
In addition to regular BOD meetings, we also organize Quarterly General Body Meetings with the IALFM community in general and for our members in particular, to provide updates on continuing and new projects planned for future. IALFM Committees provide updates on their areas of focus and the work they are undertaking to move IALFM mission forward. We request you to please attend these meetings regularly. Minutes of these meetings are shared via email with the community and are also available upon request that can be sent to IALFM Board.

As per our Constitution (Article 10.1.2), General Body Meetings are organized once every quarter on last Saturday of each quarter. Announcement is sent via email to all community members and also included in our weekly newsletter sent before Friday prayers, usually on Thursdays. Please subscribe to it (see 'Subscribe to our newsletter' section on our main landing page on our website), if you want to receive these notifications.

General Body Meetings usually start after Duhr Salah at the IALFM Masjid and/or over Zoom (when appropriate). See prayer timings on our main landing page on our website.

General Agenda Items
– Opening Prayer followed by some remarks by IALFM President
– Review of minutes from last Quarterly General Body Meeting
– Financial Update
– Staff & Other Important Updates
– Updates & Progress Reports from different IALFM Committees
– Questions & Answers Session
– Closing Prayer & Adjournment

Note: All members of the community are welcome to attend as observers, however only current IALFM members in good standing can vote on motions & other issues at the meeting, if we have required quorum (51%) to vote & make decisions on matters under discussion thru simple majority voting. See IALFM Constitution (Article 10.2.1) for relevant details.

item.title: Our Director of Education & Youth
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Ustadh Moneeb Sarmad grew up in Calgary, Canada and graduated with a Masters of Education after completing his undergrad in Electrical Engineering from the University of Calgary. In his hometown, he was involved in serving his local Muslim community in a wide range of activities such as khutbahs, halaqahs, pastoral support, and youth work. He started his journey in Islamic studies in 2010 when he began his hifdh.

Moreover, he studied fiqh, tafsir, hadith, and 'aqidah with the local scholars of his community. To further his education, he moved to Dallas, TX and graduated from the 'Alimiyyah degree at the Qalam Institute He continues to serve the Islamic Association of Lewisville and Flower Mound (IALFM) in the capacity of Director of Education and Youth, which he has been committed to full-time since March 2022.

During his free time, he enjoys taking his daughter for walks, getting lost in history books, and munching on all kinds of kababs. Amongst other things, one of his life goals is to empower the Muslim community, especially the youth to be confident and proud Muslims and become the shining light in the world we live in.

You can contact Ustadh Moneeb Sarmad at moneeb@ialfm.org

item.dc:creator: moneeb

item.title: Sunday School Charges
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
As mentioned on our Sunday School Overview page, our fee schedule is:

Fees

Monthly Tuition Fees (charged for 9 months, Sep thru May)
Tuition fee for 1st Child: $75/Month
Tuition fee for 2nd Child: $45/Month (40% discount)
Tuition fee for 3rd Child: $35/Month (53% discount)
Tuition fee for 4th Child (and more): $25/Month (67% discount)

One Time Charge (charged in August)
Registration Fee (one-time charge): $50 (for 1 Child) & $100 (for 2 or more Children)
Supply Fee: $25 per Child
1 Workbook: $17 (Pre-K & KG), $9 (1st thru 6th Grades)

Charges for families of different sizes

Following are the details of charges based on the above schedule of fees.

One Child
Registration Fee: $50 + Supply Fee: $25 = $75 (one-time fee, at the time of registration)
Monthly Tuition Fee: $75 (charged for 9 months on a recurring basis, for a total of $675)
(Cost of workbook depends on the student's grade)

Two Children
Registration Fee: $100 + Supply Fee: $50 ($25 x 2) = $150 (one-time fee, at the time of registration)
Monthly Tuition Fee: $75 + $45 = $120 (charged for 9 months on a recurring basis, for a total of $1,080)
(Cost of workbook depends on the student's grade)

Three Children
Registration Fee: $100 + Supply Fee: $75 ($25 x 3) = $175 (one-time fee, at the time of registration)
Monthly Tuition Fee: $75 + $45 + $35 = $155 (charged for 9 months on a recurring basis, for a total of $1,395)
(Cost of workbook depends on the student's grade)

Four Children
Registration Fee: $100 + Supply Fee: $100 ($25 x 4) = $200 (one-time fee, at the time of registration)
Monthly Tuition Fee: $75 + $45 + $35 + $25 = $180 (charged for 9 months on a recurring basis, for a total of $1,620)
(Cost of workbook depends on the student's grade)

Note: There is a 10% discount, only on tuition fee, when paid in full (9 months), at the beginning of the academic year. Please note that you can only make this advance full payment via Check, ACH (Bank Account), Zelle or Cash (due to no processing charges). Credit/Debit Cards cannot be used (due to payment processing charges) to pay full tuition fee in advance to avail this discount.

item.title: Feedback to BoD
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM BOD works to serve the Muslim community of Lewisville, Flower Mound & surrounding suburbs and is always willing to hear feedback from the community.

Please feel free to send feedback to BOD via email at: bod@ialfm.org

BOD will review the feedback and take appropriate actions. If a response is requested, BOD provides a response within 24 to 48 hours. During holidays the response could be delayed. BOD may take more time to respond on important matters that would require deliberation and discussion among BOD members. You will be notified as such if that happens to be the case with issue at hand.

item.title: Education Committee
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM EducationCommittee is responsible for the educational needs of the wider IALFM community, except IALFM Sunday School that is operated by Sunday School Committee.

Contact & Feedback:

You can contact Education Committee by sending an email at: education@ialfm.org

item.title: Education at IALFM
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM has two main committees that are responsible for catering to the educational needs of our community, i.e. Education & Sunday School committees.

Please see the following links for more info on these two committees and their functions.

Education Committee

Sunday School Committee

item.title: Sunday School Committee
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Sunday School Committee manages & operates the IALFM Sunday School for basic Islamic education of students in Pre-K thru 6th grades in regular schools. Sunday School Committee has the following objectives.

  1. Organize academic, social and fundraising activities in support of IALFM Sunday School
  2. Manage administrative functions of the school appropriately and effectively
  3. Recruit and train volunteers to help better utilize their individual talents, time and efforts
  4. Develop a positive and supportive atmosphere to enhance students’ educational and extracurricular experiences
  5. Promote better communication among teachers, parents and community in general on educational aspects of our school

Contact & Feedback

You can contact Sunday School Committee by sending an email at: sundayschool@ialfm.org

item.title: Quran School
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Quran School currently offers two different programs to the IALFM community for Quranic education. They are:

Quran Nazira Program

Quran Hifz Program

item.title: Quran Nazira Program
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Students from all levels (beginners to advance) are welcome to join!

Minimum required age for kids to enroll is 4 to 5 years. However, this does not guarantee an admission. Our teachers will conduct an assessment to see if a child is ready to begin Quran lessons.

Parents & Students can opt for Online or Onsite (at the masjid) class & lesson delivery option.

Our 'Nazira Program' provides Quran classes with emphasis on Tajweed for all levels. Tajweed refers to the rules governing the pronunciation aspects during recitation of Quran, just the way it was recited in the times of our Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). We provide Quran Tajweed classes for beginners to advance level students.

Quran Teachers:

Imam Rasheed Farah
Sr. Asma Qureshi
Sr. Yasmeen Almasri

Monthly Tuition Fee:

$75 for each student

Note: Due to increased costs associated with the hiring of well-qualified Quran Tajweed teachers, there are no discounts for multiple children or students from the same family.

Program Details:

Classes are usually offered from Monday thru Friday, from 3:30pm to 7:30pm

Note: Summer break timings vary every year (June thru July). Last year, the timings were from Mon to Fri, from 9am to 1pm

  • It’s a customized program to help each student individually
  • Students learn how to read & recite Quran with basic understanding of Arabic letters with Tajweed rules
  • Each online class session is around 20 minutes
  • Each student attends 3 classes per week

Registration:

Quran School Registration Form

Please complete and submit the above registration form for each student that you want to enroll in our Quran School. If you have any questions, please contact Sr Samia Babar (Quran School Administrator) via email at quranschool@ialfm.org

Quran Hifz Program

Registration for Quran Hifz program classes is open.

Following are the important details related to Hifz program.

item.title: Sunday School Overview
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Introduction

IALFM Sunday School is a non-profit school founded by the community members for the community. We have been serving the community since 2000. Our mission is to provide an age-appropriate educational program to our students from Pre-K thru 6th Grades (4 to 11 years old) based on the principles of basic primary & secondary education encapsulated in a rich Islamic atmosphere. Classes are held at our premises located at 3430 Peters Colony Road in Flower Mound, Texas. We strive to provide an enriched supportive environment through exploration, discovery, and creativity under the guidance of trained & caring staff to help foster learning in our students. Our goal is to prepare students to conduct themselves as responsible and respectable citizens of the community, the nation, and the world at large.

We work hard to serve the community by maintaining affordable tuition rates & providing need based tuition fee waivers in the form of scholarships thru ERF Program. All of this is possible through the generous help provided by volunteers from our community, who work as a team to operate our school. Almost all functions of the school are run by a team of volunteers, starting from Administrators to Teachers, Teaching Assistants & Substitutes. As an independent non-profit school, we receive no financial support from any level of government and depend entirely upon tuition fees, donations, volunteers, and fund-raising activities to balance our annual operating budget. As tuition fee income alone is not sufficient to cover the entire cost of operations, we therefore appeal to our community to assist us in maintaining and more importantly enhancing the services IALFM Sunday School provides to the community.

The long term growth and sustainability of this institution depends entirely on the generosity of our wonderful community. We invite you to explore different projects that we have to find out more about how you can get involved through the available programs. We are always looking for volunteers to actively participate in our Sunday School Committee and provide teaching & administrative services in different roles.

Levels & Graduation Requirements

The school has developed curriculum for four levels. Students attend each level for two years. Students are eligible to begin Level 1 if they are at least 4 years of age by September 1 at the start of the school academic year. Although students remain in the same level for two years, they do receive different instructions each year as we use different curriculum. Below is a description of the classes. Students graduate upon completing two years of level 4 or entering the 7th grade in their regular schools.

Levels, Age Groups & Grades

Level 1: 4 to 5 years old on September 1 (Pre-School/Pre-K & Kindergarten)
Level 2: 6 to 7 years old on September 1 (1st & 2nd Grades)
Level 3: 8 to 9 years old on September 1 (3rd & 4th Grades)
Level 4: 10 to 11 years old on September 1 (5th & 6th Grades)

For grades 7th and up, IALFM offers a new program called Pillars Academy which will replace level 5 that used to be offered by Sunday school for 7th & 8th graders.

Schedule

At present, there is only one session of our Sunday School. We have operated two sessions in the past to accommodate more students. School starts at 10:30am and ends at 1:45pm.

Study Materials

The school uses a variety of books, workbooks and other study materials from renowned scholars, including ...

Islamic Studies (Level 1), Short Surahs (Level 2), I Love Islam Series (Levels 2 & 3), The Prophets of Allah (Levels 2 & 3), Juz Amma (Levels 3 & 4) and What Islam is All About (Level 4).

Parents may buy or rent the textbooks for a single fixed annual cost (covers all textbooks for a single level). Workbooks required for each level will need to be bought for all students. Sunday school will provide all required supplies & miscellaneous study-materials to students for their classes (covered thru supply fee). All students may use a standard Sunday School Canvas Bag for study materials (Parents can buy it at the time of in-person registration or later, if/when needed).

Extra Curricular Activities

IALFM Sunday School is dedicated to providing a nourishing atmosphere with a balanced curriculum. Last year, we organized several exciting workshops for our students, includuing Calligraphy workshop, Self-Defense workshop, and Anti-Bullying workshop.

Basic Curriculum Overview

IALFM Sunday School’s curriculum is age-based and consists of topics like basic lessons in Ethics & Human values, short stories to teach basic Principles of Life, Good Qualities of a Human Being, how to establish Healthy Habits & basic Arabic language in Levels 1-3. Levels 3-4 focus more on World Cultures and Arts, the History of Islamic & other Empires of past and the Contributions of Muslims to the Modern World. Students also receive primary level instructions in reading and writing basic Arabic as a second language. As students enter higher grades, more comprehensive details on these subjects become an area of greater focus in their classes.

Level 1 (ages 4 or 5)

    *  Memorizing Surahs: Al-Fatiha, An-Nas, Al-Iqlas, Al-Falaq, Al-Kauthar, Al-Lahab, An-Nasr, Al-Kaafirun, Al-Maun, Al-Quraish, and Al-Feel
    *  Identifying and writing letters of the Arabic alphabet in their basic form
    *  Describing Attributes of Allah, the 5 pillars of Islam, and the beliefs of Islam
    *  Demonstrating basic Islamic Adab
    *  Describing the stories of some of the Prophets mentioned in the Qur’an

Level 2 (ages 6 or 7)

    *  Recitation & Tafsir of Surahs: Al-Fatiha, An-Nas, Al-Iqlas, Al-Falaq, Al-Kauthar, Al-Lahab, An-Nasr, Al-Kafirun, Al-Maun, Al-Quraish, and Al-Feel
    *  Identifying and writing letters of the Arabic alphabet in their various forms
    *  Describing stories of some of the Prophets mentioned in the Qur’an
    *  Describing the Attributes of Allah, the 5 pillars of Islam, and the beliefs of Islam
    *  Identifying deeds as Halal or Haram

Level 3 (ages 8 or 9)

    *  Recitation & Tafsir of Surahs: Al-Humaza, Al-Asr, Al-Takathur, Al-Qariah, Al-Adiyat, Al-Zalzala, Al- Bayyina, Al-Qadr, Al-Alaq, and At-Teen
    *  Sounding out words with short vowel sounds
    *  Describing the Five pillars of Islam
    *  Describing ways to worship Allah
    *  Events in the life of Prophet Muhammad (SAW)
    *  Describing ways to increase Iman
    *  Practicing Islamic adab
    *  Properties of good Islamic character
    *  Islam’s influence on cultures around the world

Level 4 (ages 10 or 11)

    *  Recitation & Tafsir of Surahs in Juz 30
    *  Phonetically sounding out words with various phonetic rules
    *  Rites associated with Salah and Ablution
    *  Strengthening an Islamic identity
    *  Reviewing the lives of the Prophets
    *  Describing the character of some of the Sahabah
    *  Describing Islam’s influence on the arts
    *  Listing months & events of the Islamic calendar

Fees

Tuition fee for 1st Child: $75/Month (charged for 9 months)
Tuition fee for 2nd Child: $45/Month (40% discount)
Tuition fee for 3rd Child: $35/Month (53% discount)
Tuition fee for 4th Child (and more): $25/Month (67% discount)
Registration Fee (one-time charge): $50 (for 1 Child) & $100 (for 2 or more Children)
Supply Fee (one-time charge): $25 per Child
Textbooks Lending Program: $10 per Child (you will need to buy workbooks)

Books
Level 1 (Pre-K & KG): $17 (1 Text+Workbook, For New Students Only)
Level 2 (1st & 2nd Grades): $10 (Textbooks Rent Program) + $25 (3 Workbooks)
Level 3 (3rd & 4th Grades): $10 (Textbooks Rent Program) + $27 (3 Workbooks)
Level 4 (5th & 6th Grades): $10 (Textbooks Rent Program) + $19 (2 Workbooks)

Payment:

  1. On the 'Registration + Orientation Day', you will be required to pay all applicable fees
  2. Fees payment can be made via:
    1. Credit/Debit Cards
    2. ACH Debit Payments from your Bank (you will need to provide your bank account info)
    3. Personal Check (if paying on a monthly basis, postdated checks are required for tuition fee payment for entire academic year at the registration time)
    4. Cash

Notes:

  1. Tuition fee also includes cost of snacks provided to children during recess
  2. Fees will also be utilized to pay for Speakers' honorarium on special events (generally two such events are planned every year), Volunteers Appreciation Initiatives & technology needs.
  3. Tuition fee is due on the 1st of each month, beginning in August, and ending in May.
  4. Late fee of $10 may be charged if tuition fee is not received by the 5th of each month. This includes declined credit/debit cards & NSF checks. Additional bank fees may also apply.
  5. There is a 10% discount, only on tuition fee, when paid in full (9 months), at the beginning of the academic year. Please note that you can only make this advance full payment via Check, ACH (Bank Account), Zelle or Cash (due to no processing charges). Credit/Debit Cards cannot be used (due to payment processing charges) to pay full tuition fee in advance to avail this discount.
  6. For families in need of any financial assistance, our masjid may provide assistance through an ERF Program. Please talk to the Sunday School Administrator if you want to apply for tuition fee assistance or send an email to: sundayschool@ialfm.org

Grading Policy

Students are awarded at an Annual Graduation Ceremony based on a combination of their performance in the following areas: Attendance, Homework Assignments completion & Test Grades. Tests are administered every semester (2 semesters), for a total of two tests a year. The overall grade is comprised of Attendance (30%), Homework Assignments (30%) & Tests (40%). For any homework assignments that are turned in late by the student, points are deducted. No make-up homework can be turned in after the date of the Semester test. Homework assignments are provided to students for the entire semester period on a sheet. Although our teachers are encouraged to notify parents and students when they are missing their homework, a lack of notification from the teacher does not constitute an excuse for missed homework. Teachers’ contacts information is provided so that parents have the opportunity to contact the teacher to ask any question about their child’s homework assignments. Report cards are also issued to communicate students’ progress.

Dress Code

Female students are expected to wear hijab inside the masjid at all times. Should a female student forget her hijab, extra hijabs are available for borrowing at the masjid. Male students are expected to wear pants and shirts with sleeves while inside the masjid. If shorts are worn by male students, parents should provide a thoub to wear on top of their clothes. Students should avoid tight clothing, tight leggings & short tops. All children are expected to dress neatly and conservatively with no pictures of people or animals on their clothes. If a student comes to school dressed inappropriately, they will be politely reminded of the dress code. However, continued failure to follow the dress code will result in a notification to parents and disciplinary actions.

Attendance Policy

Although students benefit most by attending class every Sunday, the school realizes that children sometimes become ill or have to travel with families on weekends. Therefore, students are allowed up to two excused absences in a year. An absence will be considered excused only when a written note for student is sent by parents via e-mail at sundayschool@ialfm.org. Please note that you must send the note via email before the end of the day on that Sunday that was missed by student in order for the absence to be considered excused. The parent or child is responsible for asking about make-up homework and bringing completed assignments to the teacher. Students with perfect attendance, i.e. no excused or unexcused absences will be eligible for the ‘Perfect Attendance Award’ on Graduation Day. Perfect attendance also includes participation in competitions (where applicable and notified). If a student misses a test and needs a make-up test, student or parents may approach the class-teacher or administrator to arrange for that at a mutually agreed upon time and place. Administering a make-up test is solely at teacher or administrator’s discretion.

Frequently Asked Questions for Parents

We highly recommend that all parents should also review the FAQs over here.

Staff

Our school is run entirely by volunteers from the community. Volunteers are utilized to complete the following tasks and/or fill the following positions to operate school.

    *  Classroom/Snacks Break setup, supervision & clean-up
    *  Clerical duties
    *  Classroom Assistants in Training (for 9th -12th graders)
    *  Classroom Assistants
    *  Substitute Teachers
    *  Classroom Teachers (for Religion & Quran)
    *  Sunday School Committee Members

Interested in Volunteering for Sunday School?

If you are interested in volunteering for our Sunday School, please complete & submit the Staff Registration Form on our website.

Sunday School Committee

See more info on Sunday School Committee over here.

Contact & Feedback

You can contact the Sunday School Committee or any of its members by sending an email at: sundayschool@ialfm.org

Academic Calendar

Calendar for current academic year is available here.

item.title: FAQs for Parents
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Here are some Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) for parents. Let us know if you still have any questions by sending us an email at: sundayschool@ialfm.org

How can I find out more info about your Sunday School, how its operated, its policies and other relevant details?

A detailed overview of our Sunday School along with its policies is available at: IALFM Sunday School Overview

How can I access your Code of Conduct, Liability Waiver and Medical Release Form that I need to sign?

All those documents are available here: IALFM_Sunday_School_CoC_LW_MRF (PDF) – This PDF contains IALFM Sunday School Code of Conduct, Liability Waiver & Medical Release forms. Please complete these forms (one copy per family for all children) with required details, sign & date them, and bring them with you to submit in our office on your child’s first day of school. These forms are required for student registration.

What are the policies around drop-off at school start time (10:30 am) and end time (1:45 pm)?

IALFM Sunday School starts at 10:30 am, please make sure you plan for timely drop-off. We take attendance once after the start of the first period and then once again after the start of second period. In case of delayed arrival or early leave, student will not be awarded full attendance. Delayed arrivals also affect the class discipline and make it harder for teachers to maintain class decorum during lesson delivery.

IMPORTANT: Our late pick-up policy is especially very important for parents to follow, as it relates to the safety of our children.

a. Sunday School ends at 1:45 pm, parents should be in the parking lot at that time (at 1:45 pm), no exceptions!

b. In case you are running late, we allow you to be there before 2:15 pm in all such cases (which gives you half an hour/30 mins to pick up your kids).

c. Running late beyond 2:15 pm is not acceptable and is treated as an exception, only for emergency reasons (read more about it below).

d. Administrators and others at Sunday School are all volunteers and will leave the masjid premises at 2:15 pm.

e. In the special case when you are running late for more than 30 mins (beyond 2:15pm), IF you want Administrators/others at Sunday School to still be there with your kids, you have the responsibility to notify the Administrators in advance by phone/text/email (whichever works best for you) about your situation so that necessary steps can be taken in that regard. (also note that you do not need to notify us, if you do not want us to remain there after 2:15 pm with your kids, in case you feel comfortable with your kids to be there on their own).

f. In this exceptional scenario, when parents are running late for more than half an hour (beyond 2:15pm), Administrator or anyone at the Sunday School is not responsible for the safety of your kids, if you do not notify us in advance.

We have had a significant number of such cases where multiple families/parents did not notify us in advance, while some others did notify in advance (which is much appreciated). Starting from August 2020, all such situations will result in a monetary fine for parents. We will devise a policy & notify parents about it before next school year starts in August 2020.

What is your policy for handling students with allergies?

We handle these situations based on requests from parents and availability of resources. Parents are requested to notify the Administrator in writing (send an email at sundayschool@ialfm.org) about this situation with appropriate details at the time of registration. On specific days during allergy season, parents are also requested to send a note to teachers (copy to Administrators at above email address) requesting them to keep their child inside, when he/she is not supposed to go outside, be it during normal school hours or only during the break. Please take time to directly talk to the teachers and their assistants as well when you are dropping off your children. We need to work together to make this work for our children. Also, if the child himself/herself knows that he/she is not supposed to go outside on certain days/times, then that would be the best defense for him/her to avoid allergies, in case others are not around or forget it (due to momentary lapse).

What is your policy on student pictures or videos taken at an event or portraits at the end of school year?

All pictures & videos are taken by our assigned volunteer photographers and then shared with the parents thru an email after storing them securely in a shared album in our Office 365 based secured storage or in unlisted state (can only be viewed thru direct link, not available publicly on YouTube) thru our masjid’s YouTube account. The picture albums or videos are shared for 2 weeks only for viewing and download purposes (so that parents can view and/or download & save their children’s pictures/videos), within the same group of students, parents and volunteers with whom your child is attending our Sunday School. The picture album or video sharing is disabled after 2 weeks as that is deemed enough time for parents to view or download them. If you do not want any individual pictures or videos of your children to be taken and/or shared in an album, please notify the Administrator in advance in writing by sending an email at sundayschool@ialfm.org. Its not possible for us to exclude individual kids from group pictures or videos. If you wish so, please ask your kids to not participate in group pictures and videos, that’d be the best way to ensure they are not captured in pictures or videos at all. We will try our best to take appropriate steps to respect your request on individual picture/video sharing. Also, its best to talk to teachers, assistants and administrators about it on the day of event when pictures are being taken, to remind them about your request to ensure appropriate steps are taken. We are a volunteer-run school so cooperation from parents is always requested to help us operate it in the best possible ways.

Do you have any resources to work with children with special needs?

Sorry, we do not have any trained teachers in our Sunday School who can teach and take care of children with special needs. We handle such situations on a best-effort basis.

Note: This is a living document. We will keep adding new info for parents on this page.

item.title: Sunday School Staff Registration
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Our Sunday School is run solely by volunteers. We have different positions available depending on your educational background, skills, experience & availability. If you are interested in volunteering for our Sunday School, please complete the registration form below after reading the following instructions.

Please read the important points below before submitting registration form.

IMPORTANT:
1. All interested individuals should register by completing & submitting this form by Sunday August 3rd, 2025. Interviews for prospective candidates will be held virtually.
2. All classes will be offered in-person. Classes start on Sunday August 17th, 2025.
3. Sunday school timings is from 10:30am to 1:45pm. See 'Calendar' at: https://www.ialfm.org/ss-calendar
4. We have two 75-minute periods, with a recess of 30 minutes in between the two periods. Dhuhr Salah will be at 1:30 pm, followed by dismissal at 1:45 pm. See 'Daily Schedule' at: https://www.ialfm.org/ss-overview
5. Final placement for each position will be assigned by the Sunday School Committee.
6. Have questions? Send us an e-mail at sundayschool@ialfm.org

Staff Registration form: https://forms.office.com/r/dncmvvcXKa (direct link) or use the form below.
