item.title: Youth
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Youth Committee

IALFM Youth group is also known as iFLY!

Do Good and Be Guided!

IALFM Youth Committee caters to the growing needs of the local youth community. Brothers and sisters who are attending school anywhere from middle school to the college or university level require nourishment for the soul and body that can only be found in a vibrant and strong Islamic community. IALFM Youth promotes excellence and achievement while promoting good, Muslim character in our youth by instilling community, spiritual and Islamic virtues. We hope to serve those needs, as well as adapt and grow to create a fun, energizing, and refreshing youth culture around the masjid – one that creates, strengthens, and motivates the future leaders of the Muslim Ummah.

Youth Committee plans many activities targeted towards youth including:

– Youth Halaqahs
– Annual Youth Picnic
– Qiyam & Iftaar
– Annual Mothers Appreciation Brunch
– Art classes
– Sports activities
– Inter-faith programs
– Volunteering at events for local charities, for IALFM ‘Feed the Neighbors’ program, and other programs

Like IALFM Youth group on Facebook!
Email: youth@ialfm.org

Youth Committee Information

The purpose of the IALFM Youth Committee is to help carry out work that supports the strategic and planned goals of iFLY, volunteering to help throughout the year is an excellent way to serve. Youth Committee was established to help plan and organize the work for the youth members of IALFM comunnity that are between the ages of 12 to 25 years.

Youth Committee members (list) are regularly assigned specific tasks to complete. The groups members may:

– want to give back to the community, fellow youth, and to help iFLY achieve its goals
– have the necessary time to commit to devote to the work of the group
– be skilled in planning, organizing and conducting work, sometimes under shortage of time, while juggling school, work, homework and/or other responsibilities
– enjoy working in a team environment for the sake of Allah (SWT)

To learn more about the responsibilities of Youth Committee members, see details below:

FAQs

I’m new to this youth group and have questions!

Q. When are appointments made?
A. Selection is made (majority voting of the current committee members) in January. Per IALFM Constitution, all committees are disbanded yearly, with the possibility to be re-appointed for another year based on Board’s decision.

Q. Do I have to be a youth group to be on a committee ?
A. Yes, you must be a active member of the youth group and have shown a regular level of participation and responsibility.

Q. What are the benefits of serving on a committee or appointed group?
A. There are many benefits to serving on a iFLY committee or group! To name a few, serving for sake of Allah ( SWT) , development of leadership skills, chance to effect change, expanded networking opportunities, resume building, the opportunity to give back to your community, and recognition as a leader.
The youth committee is to help carry out work that supports the goals of iFLY, volunteering is an excellent way to serve your community.

Q. How do I apply for youth committee?
A. You contact the youth committee by email at: youth@ialfm.org. You must express why you’re interested in serving. Volunteer apps are collected for selection from February to December. The selection is made in January.
The purpose of a youth committee is to help carry out work that supports the strategic and planned goals of iFLY, volunteering to help throughout the year is an excellent way to serve.

Q. What is the length of time that I’d be serving on an appointed group?
A. Committee members serve one (1) year. Per IALFM Constitution, all committees are disbanded yearly, with the possibility to be re-appointed for another year based on Board’s decision and active participation. Individuals can become inactive at anytime due to school or work commitments .

Q. As a iFLY committee member, can I serve on an other IALFM committee?
A. Yes, you can join on any other committees!

Q. What qualifications do I need to have?
A. No qualifications necessary, just enthusiasm. Be an active member of the youth group. Bring your ideas, commitment and serving for the sake of Allah SWT.

Q. What is the expected time commitment for group work?
A. It varies month to month, it is usually about 2-4 hours a month. We meet about every 2 weeks for 1 hour. When there are activities scheduled, we may meet 1x per week for planning purposes.

item.title: Sisters Committee
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
The IALFM Sisters Committee focuses on expanding masjid activities while providing classes on Tajweed, Hadeeth, and Halaqahs. The Sisters Committee strives to spread Islamic values within the women of the masjid while also influencing the Flower Mound Muslim community. Through weekly and monthly programs, the committee brings scholars from around the area while arranging events to make the masjid a focal point of engagement.

Goals

*  To strive to enable and promote sisterhood and unity among sisters irrespective of their ethnic, nationality, social or cultural backgrounds.
*  To strive to enable and facilitate learning opportunities for sisters as well as to community to strengthen their Islamic knowledge and values.
*  To strive to instill Islamic and social environment to foster Islamic values, conduct, respect and love for each other.

Activities

*  Sisters’ Halaqah
*  Weekly Taleemul Quran class for sisters ( English and Urdu)
*  Sisters’ Fitness Walk program
*  Inviting female scholars for sisters conference / workshop
*  Picnic for sisters (twice a year)
*  Hadeeth Class (open to all community)

Programs and Events

*  Monthly Collection of Food Items for ICNA Food Pantry
*  Bi monthly Volunteering at Helping Hand Warehouse
*  Bi monthly Volunteering at ICNA Food Pantry

Contact

To contact the Sisters Committee about upcoming events, joining the committee, or any other questions, feel free to send an email at: sisters@ialfm.org

item.title: Event Request
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Want to hold an event at IALFM Masjid?

Please use the information and forms on this page to submit a request for IALFM BOD/Committee approval.

  1. Event Request Form  (click to open the form, complete & submit it)
  2. Change Request for Scheduled Events  (click to open the form, complete & submit it)
  3. Submit a new or updated poster/ad for your event by sending it via email to events@ialfm.org

Notes:

  1. Someone from IALFM BOD/Committee will get back to you via email in a few days after you submit a new event request form.
  2. If you do not get any response after 3 days, feel free to send us an email at events@ialfm.org, we will follow up on your request.
  3. Let us know if you have any feedback on this process or forms, send us an email at: events@ialfm.org

***********************************************

How to use Projector (Room 1)

Projector’s remote control and a VGA cable (for laptop connectivity) is inside the dais that our Imam or Friday Khateeb uses (usually located inside/near the minbar), look in top drawer. A wooden rod to bring down the projector screen is located inside the minbar, look in the space on left hand side.

Using remote you turn the projector on, use the VGA cable to connect to laptop (VGA port is required on laptop or you will need a converter), other end of it goes into VGA port on the wall and screen will tell you when the connection has been made (hour glass shows up for a few seconds and then picture loads up of what you have on your laptop screen) and projector will start projecting, otherwise it projects, ‘no signal‘ on screen.

To turn the projector off, press the off button on remote control twice in succession.

Happy projecting!

Any questions, send us an email at: events@ialfm.org

item.title: Volunteer Signup
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Want to Volunteer in IALFM Committees?

IALFM BOD is always looking for community members to work in voluntary positions to help different committees working for the welfare of our community!

Please send us an email and we will add you to our WhatsApp group for Volunteers!

If you're a member of our IALFM Community or Announcements WhatsApp group, then you can also join 'IALFM Volunteers' WhatsApp group on your own, its listed within IALFM Community (under 'Groups you can join' section) ...

Any questions, concerns or feedback? Please send us an email ...

item.title: Emergency Relief Fund (ERF)
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Emergency Relief Fund (ERF) program has been established at IALFM to provide temporary emergency financial assistance to our deserving community members in their crucial times of need and is sustained thru the Zakat, Sadaqa & Zakat al-Fitr ('Fitra' during Ramadan) donations from people within our community. The program is run by a committee of volunteers from our community, who have instituted an application and verification process to maintain required Islamic standards in tune with local laws and principles followed by other similar Islamic charity 501(c)(3) organizations in the larger DFW metropolitan area.

This is a prime example of members of community stepping up to help other members in their time of need to enable them to hopefully play a productive role in future upbringing of our community.

How to apply?

In order to apply for financial assistance thru IALFM's ERF Program, please fill out the online application form that is linked below and submit it for processing. We do not use any paper-based application, so there is nothing to print and fill out.

ERF APPLICATION FORM (click on this link to open the form)

Complete the form and then submit it for processing. Please provide required supporting documents with your application, especially last three months of your bank account statement.

Note: We take 3 to 5 business days to process your application and provide a response to you via email or at your provided phone number. If you don't get a response after a week of submitting your application, please contact us for an update via email, see our email address below.

Important: Applicants need to be legal permanent residents (Citizen, Green Card Holders, etc.) of US to be considered for assistance. We do not provide assistance with any payments that include interest, like Mortgage, Car Lease, etc. We also do not assist with tuition fee payments. You may reach out to another organization (A Continuous Charity Foundation), that provides such assistance to US residents.

——————————————————————————–

Questions or Feedback

You can contact the ERF Committee by sending an email at: erf@ialfm.org

item.title: COVID-19: Masjid is Currently Open for Prayers
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded: March 2021 Update IALFM Board's Message to Community: We understand you may have questions regarding how Governor Abbott’s Executive Order impacts IALFM and its current policies. While the Governor’s executive order removes the statewide mask mandate (effective March 10, 2021), it does not overturn organizational policies regarding masks. Also note that the order does not eliminate social distancing. Please refer to #4 in the Executive Order from Governor Abbott. At this time there is no change to IALFM current policies regarding masks and social distancing. Masks and social distancing are required for all those attending prayers at IALFM inside or outside the masjid facility, for daily or Friday prayers. Jazak Allah Khair, IALFM Board of Directors ------------------------------------------------- Nov. 2020 Announcement Masjid is currently Open for regular and Friday prayers The Friday prayer will start 1:45pm every Friday. Prayers will be in the courtyard or lawn outside masjid with social distancing. IMPORTANT: IF YOU DO NOT FEEL WELL, AND YOU HAVE SYMPTOMS LIKE HIGH GRADE FEVER, COUGH, etc., PLEASE DO NOT COME TO THE MASJID. STAY & PRAY AT YOUR HOME! REQUIREMENTS: All members of the community who plan to attend any of the prayers at the masjid, will be required to: a) Do Wudu at their homes b) Wear face masks c) Bring their own prayer rugs If any member is non-compliant with any of these requirements, he/she shall be asked to leave the Masjid’s property immediately! We really appreciate your utmost cooperation in this regard. We pray to Allah for the safety of our congregation & fellow human beings - Aameen! Let us know via email if you have any questions/concerns. IALFM Board of Directors

item.title: Call for Volunteers: Sunday School Administrator
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded: Sunday School is an integral and important part of the IALFM masjid. It provides our children the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of Islam. You can read more about the Sunday School here. We are calling for volunteers to help fill the role of the Sunday School Administrator/Principal for the year starting in August 2020. We are looking for passionate, motivated, and committed individuals who can take ownership and lead this school to the next level and provide everlasting impact on our youth and community. Read more about this position and requirements over here. Please contact IALFM Board via email, if you're interested.

item.title: IALFM Membership Renewal
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
This page provides instructions on how to signup or renew IALFM Membership on an annual basis.

Article 3 of the IALFM Constitution provides info on membership eligibility & other relevant details (see screenshot below).

There are multiple ways to sign-up or renew your membership, online or in-person at the masjid, please review further details below.

How To Sign-up For IALFM Membership
Please see instructions below that are appropriate to your situation.

Sign-up or Renew Family Membership (Online)
Please use the link below to sign-up for new or renew your existing Family Membership by paying $50 using your credit, debit card or bank account.

Family Membership (click on the link to proceed)

Sign-up or Renew Individual Membership (Online)
Please use the link below to sign-up for new or renew your existing Individual Membership by paying $25 using your credit, debit card or bank account.

Individual Membership (click on the link to proceed)

Sign-up or Renew Membership at the Masjid (Kiosks)
Keep in mind that new masjid building is open for Friday prayers. You can sign-up for new or renew your membership in person using the MOHID Kiosks or Terminals installed at our new masjid building. There is one terminal on each side in the foyer at the entrance. Click on the Donate button on the main screen & then select the appropriate membership level (Family or Individual) and make payment using your credit or debit card.

Sign-up or Renew Membership at the Masjid (Check/Cash Payment)
You can download & complete the Membership Form (click on the link to download it), submit it in Operations donation box inside new masjid building (in the foyer at the entrance) with required fees (cash or check) and we will process that for you. New masjid building is open during Friday prayers.

Have any Comments or Questions? Please contact us at: membership@ialfm.org

item.title: Funeral Prayers
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM has established a Funeral Committee to provide related assistance to our community. We do not charge for this assistance (see the specifics below) and is available to everyone in our community. However, there may be charges associated to Funeral Home, where body of the deceased is prepared for the funeral and Muslim Cemetery, where body of the deceased is laid to rest.

How to receive assistance with a funeral?
Please send an email to our Funeral Committee at funeral@ialfm.org to get started (as early as possible) - someone from that committee will then contact you directly, to guide & help thru the process.

What happens at IALFM premises for a funeral service?
1. Funeral committee will work with you to arrange for an appropriate day/time for Funeral prayers (Salat-ul-Janazah).

2. We will also help with the arrangements for a private viewing at the masjid, if the family of the deceased wishes to do so.

3. We will also provide guidance on transportation mechanics to facilitate funeral prayers and private viewing.

4. Our religious leader, Imam Rasheed Farah will be available for counseling, if needed, at the request of family of the deceased.

5. We will also help with community announcements for funeral prayer and other events, like Azza & Dua, at the request of family of the deceased.

item.title: Pillars Academy
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
About Pillars Academy

Pillars Academy aims to empower Muslim youth in their faith and identity. This comprehensive program provides a holistic educational experience, incorporating academic knowledge, Islamic values, and traditions. With an emphasis on inclusivity, engagement, and modern teaching methodologies, the program seeks to create a nurturing environment where Muslim youth can freely express their identities, engage in dialogue, and develop critical thinking skills.

CLICK HERE TO REGISTER

Objectives

We aim to serve the educational needs of our community to achieve the following key objectives:

  1. Foster a strong Islamic identity among students by instilling a sense of pride, understanding, and connection to their faith.
    • We aim to achieve this by teaching our students the pillars of an Islamic identity:
      1. Creed - what we believe and why we believe in it
      2. Divine Ethics - a dynamic framework on how to approach life’s daily challenges and interpersonal interactions
      3. Worship rooted in the Love, Hope, and Fear of Allāh ﷻ - a thorough understanding of Islāmic concepts of the purification of the soul and ritual worship (Ṭahārah, Ṣalāh, etc…)
  2. Provide opportunities for students to develop leadership skills within the context of Islamic values. Through identification, training, and responsibility, students are empowered to take on leadership roles within the school and community such as volunteering at local mosques, organizing charitable events, or engaging in interfaith dialogue, serving as tutors etc…

Program Description

Our program aims to provide comprehensive Islamic education to students in a structured and engaging environment.

  • We offer the program to two age groups: Middle School (grades 7-8 / 12-14 years) and High School (grades 9-12 / 15-18 years).
  • In its full capacity, the Middle School component is will be a two-year journey, allowing students to progress through a comprehensive curriculum at a comfortable pace. The High School component extends over four years as well, providing a deeper understanding of Islamic principles and practices while preparing students for higher education and leadership roles.
  • We only hire teachers who are educated in Islamic Sciences and possess the expertise to provide relevant and engaging instruction tailored to the needs of our youth.
  • Pillars Academy follows a semester-based system with two semesters per year: Fall and Spring. Each semester consists of three courses, making a total of six courses taught throughout the year.
  • For the year 2025-2026, classes will be on Saturdays from 11am-2pm. Take a look at a detailed overview of Pillars Academy Schedule & Courses.
  • Monthly Fee is $65 per student, charged for 9 months for a cumulative final total of $585 per year for 2 semesters. There is no discount for siblings.
  • In order to learn more about how the PA is run and managed, please consult the Student-Parent Handbook.

Questions

Please send us an email ...

Academic Calendar

Pillars Academy's School Calendar is based on the IALFM Sunday School & LISD's calendar. Below is this year's calendar (tentative) ...

item.title: Multi-Purpose Hall Schedule
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
All of buildings and facilities were constructed for use of IALFM members and to meet the needs of our congregation by the grace of Allah SWT. Therefore, every facility is to be treated respectfully at all times. At no time are the buildings or facilities of IALFM to be used by persons or groups for any questionable activities. The MPH (Multi-Purpose Hall/Men's Prayer Hall), SPH (Sisters' Prayer Hall) or Classrooms will not be available for use on Friday, Eid, or during Ramadan. When you see someone praying in the halls, please keep noise levels to a minimum so as not to distract them. Activities sponsored by IALFM will take precedence and priority.

Please contact Facility Committee via email (facility@ialfm.org) for bookings (in 30-minute blocks).

See Important Guidelines below for more details.

Important Guidelines

  1. Please contact Facility Committee via email (facility@ialfm.org) for bookings (in 30-minute blocks), or in case of any scheduling conflict or improper use of MPH.
  2. It is NOT allowed for an individual or a group to use the SPH, MPH, Classrooms or Kitchen (any space on our masjid property), unless permission has been obtained in advance from the IALFM Facility Committee via email.
  3. All activities in MPH or SPH must stop 10 minutes before Iqamah. And can resume 10 minutes after Jamaat/Khateera/Lectures are completed.
  4. Community members can rent MPH, SPH or Classrooms for their events (applies only to Non-Sports-based events, like Nikah, etc., for all types of Sports activities, please see below for rules & fees). A rental fee of $100 per hour will be charged to cover operating costs (Total Fees: Number of reservation/rental hours x $100). A $250 deposit will be required one week before the scheduled event. The deposit will be returned if there is no damage to the IALFM property and no additional cleanup is required. Please read, complete and submit these forms to Facility Committee via email: Rental Agreement & Liability Waiver
  5. If you need info on how to access any equipment, please send an email to Facility Committee.
  6. MPH is closed during the Islamic month of Ramadan and not available on Fridays & Eid days.
  7. There is to be no use of tobacco, drugs or alcohol on the masjid property. Dress should be modest, appropriate for the activity, and not offensive to Islamic values. Shirts are required at all times. Improper language will not be tolerated.
  8. Participants are to wear clean, soft soled sneakers that will not mark the playing floor.
  9. The equipment room and gym floor is to be organized and cleaned after each activity and debris put into trash cans. All equipment taken from the room for use is to be returned and stored properly. This room is to be kept locked when not in use.
  10. IALFM does reserve the right to decline any request for the use of its facility if it is determined that those activities hinder IALFM programs.

    After all activities, all lights in the gym - including restrooms, equipment rooms, etc. are to be turned off. All sports and cleaning equipment, etc. are to be stored after each use. All garbage is to be properly disposed of in the trash cans. MPH, SPH, Kitchen and classrooms are to be left neat and in order. Don’t simply finish the activity and turn off the doors and lights!

    The Golden Rule: Leave it as good or better than you found it!

LIABILITY: The use of the MPH, SPH, Classrooms, and any equipment will be at the risk of the participant. IALFM does not assume liability or responsibility for any injury to any user of the IALFM facility. IALFM does not make any express or implied warranty of the premises, the equipment or fixtures. IALFM is not responsible for lost, stolen or broken personal property.

Sports Activities: Effective Immediately as of 2/2/2025

Important: Please read below for changes in the fees and use of the MPH facility for Sport Activities
Notification and Approval: All sports activities require either an email notification + approval sent to facility@ialfm.org or prior approval from the Facility Committee (with a notification in the Facility Committee WhatsApp group) to ensure there are no conflicts with planned IALFM activities.

Fees for Sports Activities: A fee of $10/adult/day and $5/youth/day applies to all sports activities. Activities planned and approved by the Youth Committee are exempt from fees for participants. However, the fee for adults remains applicable in all cases.

Fee Cap for Groups: For groups with more than 10 participants, the maximum fee is capped at $100/day. For example: A group of 10 adults will pay $100/day. Similarly, 20 youth participants will pay $100/day, and any additional youth players will not incur further fees.

Indemnification Form: All participants must sign an indemnification form, See: Liability Waiver . This fee structure does not apply to MPH use for non-sports related activities and events. 

Fees for Tournaments and Special Use (Training camps, etc.): Use of MPH facility for tournaments, training sessions and other similars activities will be assessed by the board and facility committee on a per use basis. 

Facility Condition: Players must ensure the facility is left clean and in its original condition. Failure to do so will result in suspension from future use of the facility. 

Liability for Damages: Any physical damage to the facility must be repaired by IALFM, with the responsible player(s) covering the repair costs.

Payment Compliance: Players who fail to pay the required fees will not be allowed to participate in future games.

Please contact Facility Committee via email (facility@ialfm.org) for questions!

item.title: IALFM Mobile App - Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: secretary
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Prayer Times – Privacy Policy

Last Updated: February 18, 2026

This Privacy Policy explains how IALFM Prayer Times (“we”, “us”, “our”) collects and uses information. The app is published by the Islamic Association of Lewisville and Flower Mound (IALFM). We designed this app to collect as little data as possible while providing accurate Salah times, announcements, and optional prayer notifications.

1. Information We Collect

1) Device & App Information (Non‑Personal)

We collect basic technical information such as:

  • Device model & operating system version
  • App version
  • Anonymous instance identifiers
  • Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) token used only to deliver notifications

This information cannot identify you personally.

2) Firebase Analytics (Anonymous Usage Metrics)

We use Firebase Analytics to collect anonymous, aggregated usage data such as:

  • Which screens are viewed
  • App feature usage
  • Performance and stability metrics

Firebase Analytics does NOT collect your name, email, phone number, precise location, contacts, photos, advertising identifiers, or any personally identifiable information.

3) Firebase Crashlytics (Anonymous Crash Reports)

We use Firebase Crashlytics to understand and fix crashes. Crash reports may include:

  • Device model & OS version
  • Anonymous crash logs & stack traces
  • App version installed

No personal information is collected.

4) Timezone Only (No GPS Location)

To calculate Salah times, the app reads your device’s timezone. We do not collect or access GPS location. Weather data uses a fixed Dallas-area coordinate, not your physical location.

5) Remote Config & Announcements

The app fetches non-personal announcements and configuration values from Firebase Remote Config. This data is not tied to any individual user.

6) Notifications (Opt‑In)

If you enable notifications:

  • Prayer reminders are scheduled locally on your device
  • A Firebase token is used solely to deliver notifications
  • Notification preferences are stored only on your device

7) Local Preferences & Offline Files

The following items are stored only on your device:

  • Alert and notification settings
  • Text size and UI preferences
  • Offline prayer schedule files

8) Network Requests

The app may request:

  • Weather data from Open‑Meteo
  • Prayer schedule updates from Firebase Storage
  • External links (Instagram, Facebook, Mohid donations)

These services operate under their own privacy policies.

2. Information We Do NOT Collect

  • No name, email, or phone number
  • No account or login information
  • No precise or background location
  • No contacts, photos, or device files
  • No advertising ID or ad identifiers
  • No payment information

3. How We Use Information

We use collected data solely to:

  • Display accurate Salah times
  • Deliver optional notifications
  • Improve app performance and reliability
  • Maintain secure and stable app functionality

We do not sell, rent, share, or use data for targeted advertising.

4. Third‑Party Services Used

  • Firebase Analytics – anonymous usage metrics
  • Firebase Crashlytics – crash reporting
  • Firebase Messaging – notification delivery
  • Firebase Remote Config – announcements and configuration
  • Firebase Storage – prayer schedule updates
  • Open‑Meteo – weather data
  • Mohid – donation processing (outside the app)
  • Instagram/Facebook – external links

5. Data Security

We use reputable service providers (Google Firebase, Apple, Android) and reasonable technical safeguards to protect non‑personal data. No sensitive personal data is stored or transmitted.

6. Your Choices

  • Enable or disable notifications in system settings
  • Choose whether to follow external links
  • Uninstall the app to delete all local app data

7. Children’s Privacy

IALFM Prayer Times is a general‑audience religious utility app. It does not collect personal data or use personalized advertising.

8. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The updated version will be posted on this page with a revised date.

9. Contact Us

Islamic Association of Lewisville and Flower Mound (IALFM)
Email: ialfm.app.adm@gmail.com
Website: https://ialfm.org

item.title: IALFM Mobile App Support
item.dc:creator: secretary
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Mobile App — Help & Support

Official support page for the IALFM (Islamic Association of Lewisville–Flower Mound) mobile app. Find quick fixes, privacy details, and ways to contact us.

Last updated: March 4, 2026

Need help fast?

  • Troubleshoot notifications
  • FAQs
  • Contact & feedback

Key pages

  • Privacy policy (summary)
  • Disclaimer & licensing
  • About the app

Support

General feedback & ideas

Email: ialfm.app.adm@gmail.com

Feature requests, bugs, UI suggestions.

Board & governance

Email: bod@ialfm.org

Non‑technical matters, governance questions.

Troubleshooting & Quick Fixes

Notifications aren’t arriving (iPhone/iPad)
  • Open Settings → Notifications → IALFM → turn on Allow Notifications, then enable Banners and Sounds as desired.
  • Open the IALFM app → More → Settings → ensure alerts are enabled inside the app.
  • Restart the device, then open the app once to register for push.
Prayer times look out of date
  • Pull to refresh on the main prayer times screen.
  • Ensure the device has a data connection; the app caches schedules for offline use and updates when new monthly tables are available.
Hijri date is off by a day
  • Go to More → Settings → adjust the Hijri offset (±1 day).
  • Admins may occasionally push a corrected offset so the community stays aligned.
Announcements badge (red dot) stays on
  • Visit the Announcements tab once; the dot clears after viewing the latest post.
  • If it persists, force‑quit and reopen the app.

FAQs

Do I need an account to use the app?

No. The app does not require sign‑in and stores only a few non‑identifying preferences on your device (theme, language, Hijri offset).

Who sends announcements and alerts?

Announcements are posted by IALFM admins. Urgent notices may trigger a push alert; you can manage notifications anytime in iOS Settings.

Does the app collect personal data?

No personal or sensitive personal data is collected. See the privacy summary below for details.

Privacy Policy (Summary)

  • No personal data collected. We do not collect personal CPNI or sensitive personal data.
  • On‑device settings only. Theme, language, and Hijri offset are stored locally on your device.
  • Notifications are optional. Used solely for masjid announcements; manage in iOS Settings.
  • No selling or sharing of personal data.

Read the full policy: https://www.ialfm.org/ialfm-mobile-app-privacy-policy/
Questions? Email ialfm.app.adm@gmail.com.

Disclaimer & Licensing

Important: This app is built and maintained by community volunteers and is provided as‑is, without warranties of any kind. IALFM and volunteer developer(s) are not responsible for misuse of the app, for any present or future security vulnerabilities, or for issues that may arise if the app is not updated when updates are provided. If you do not agree with these terms, please uninstall the app.

Licensing: The app is free for the IALFM community. Redistribution or commercial distribution is not permitted without written permission from IALFM.

About the App

  • Name: IALFM App
  • Focus: Daily Salah times, announcements, and community updates for the Islamic Association of Lewisville & Flower Mound.
  • Version: 1.0.0
  • Built by: Community volunteers (unpaid)
  • Contact: ialfm.app.adm@gmail.com · bod@ialfm.org

Need more help? Email ialfm.app.adm@gmail.com

Back to top ↑

item.title: IALFM Board Election Results (Term: 2020-21)
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded: Alhamdolillah, the IALFM election for four (4) available positions in the Board of Directors (BoD) has concluded on Dec 26, followed by manual voting at the IALFM masjid post the Jummah/Friday prayers this past Friday (12/27). The Election Commission has finished processing the results and has declared the following four candidates successful to serve on the IALFM BoD for 2020-21 term: Amir Ehtisham (with 22.50% votes) Ali Khokhar (with 21.70% votes) Kazal Ahmed (with 19.60% votes) Maha Ali (with 18.80% votes) The IALFM Board of Directors with new members shall go in effect on Jan 1, 2020. If you have any questions, please reach out to: election@ialfm.org or bod@ialfm.org ______________________________________________ Br. Babar Bhatti, Br. Faiz Ahmad & Sr. Sadaf Khan IALFM Election Commission IALFM Board Election (Term: 2020-21)

item.title: IALFM Friday Prayer Excuse Requirements
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
In order to enable more students from our community to join the Friday Prayer at the IALFM, the board has released the following requirements which must be met in order to obtain approval:

  1. A note from parents that their son/daughter needs to be excused (date/time).  (Parents responsibility)
  2. A personalized Friday Prayer Excuse Letter from IALFM with student name and date/time of service attended with signature from the authority at IALFM (in this case every Friday for every student attended) – linked here.
Please complete the below noted section in the excuse letter:
    1. Student full name:
    2. Confirmed service attended date and time:
    3. Signature will be open to whomever will be available that Friday from BoD members to sign the letter for each student.
Disclaimer: Regardless of all the above notes, there will be times when LISD will deny the excused leave if the student attendance falls beyond the required percentage of attendance or cases of certain days in which attendance is mandatory.
The LISD attendance policy can be accessed here.

Friday Family Night & Potluck Dinner is back!

IALFM is pleased to announce that we are resuming our Family Night & Potluck program. Starting on Fri Feb 25th, it will take place on last Friday of every month.

Below is the schedule and details for Feb's event, In Shaa Allah ...

What: Family Night Potluck
When: Friday, Feb 25th
Topic: Community Engagement
Speaker: Br Moneeb Sarmad
Program Details: See Poster Below (Dinner: 7pm, Isha: 8pm, Program: 8:15pm)

Please plan on attending with your families and bring a dish to share with others!

Questions or Comments: education@ialfm.org

item.title: Eid al-Adha is on Friday June 6!
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Eid al-Adha is on Friday June 6, 2025!

Eid-al-Adha Prayers will take place inside the new Masjid building.

1st Prayer
7:00am - Takbeerat
7:30am - Prayer

2nd Prayer
8:30am - Takbeerat
9:00am - Prayer

Parking will be available at our masjid and Flower Mound High School + Tennis Courts.

Important: Please park responsibly. Do not park in our neighboring Presbyterian Church's assigned employee parking lots, and do not park in the fire lane or loading zones in the Masjid parking lot.

We really appreciate your utmost cooperation in this regard. We pray to Allah for a great celebration & experience for everyone in our community on the day of Eid – Aameen!

Let us know via email if you have any questions/concerns.

Jazak Allah Khair!
IALFM Board of Directors

Please join us with your family and friends to celebrate Eid-al-Fitr. As usual, we will have two prayers to accommodate the needs of our growing community.

item.title: Ramadan 2022 @ IALFM
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Assalam-o-Alaikum!
As per the official calendar of Fiqh Council of North America,
this year the first day of Ramadan is on Saturday, April 2, 2022, Insha’Allah.

Community Iftar:
Click here to sign up to Community Iftar every week on Friday, Saturday & Sunday (see poster below)

Donate for Ramadan Programs:
Click here to donate for Ramadan Programs @ IALFM (to sponsor Ramadan Iftar Community Dinner on 27th Ramadan, Lailatul Qadr, and many other activities)

Ramadan Activities:
Please follow our 'IALFM Masjid' page on Facebook for all Ramadan programs & activities!
See poster below for a quick view of all activities.

Important Dates:
First Tarawih prayer will be offered on the night of Friday April 1st.
First day of fasting is on Saturday, April 2.
Last ten nights of Ramadan start on Thursday, April 21.
27th night of Ramadan (Lailatul Qadr) is on Wednesday, April 27.
Eid-ul-Fitr is on Monday, May 2, prayer will start @ 8:30am.

Zakatul Fitr is $12 per person this year. You can pay it here.

We pray to Allah (swt) for His blessings and mercy during the auspicious month of Ramadan!

Ramadan Calendar is displayed below. Here's a printable format (PDF) version.

Any questions or comments: ramadan@ialfm.org or bod@ialfm.org

item.title: IALFM Annual Community Picnic 2019 | Sun Apr 28 (11am through 3pm)
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Annual Community Picnic 2019 will be held on Sunday, April 28 from 11 am through 3 pm.

IALFM Board has decided that this year, there will be no fundraising during Picnic!

Insha Allah, Picnic will be an event to relax, enjoy good food, have fun with family & friends & meet new folks who have recently joined our community!

You can buy tickets online in advance of the event to avoid lines & ensure a quick entry with bands (for food & beverages) and ‘Picnic Bucks‘ (needed for games & activities).

Click on the ‘Buy Tickets‘ button above to buy tickets & picnic bucks online using your Credit Card or eCheck/ACH.

ONLY if you buy in advance, you’ll receive a significant discount on Picnic Bucks, i.e. 12 Bucks for $10 and 25 Bucks for $20.

Picnic Bucks will be $1 each on the day of the picnic.

We highly recommend you to buy the $20 Picnic Bucks package.

All money raised thru ticket sales at the picnic will go towards the operational & construction costs of our Masjid!

Our sincere thanks to you for being a part of this event and activities.

GAMES & ACTIVITIES THIS YEAR

Yes – We really want EVERYONE to come & have fun – Adults, Kids & especially our Youth! It should not be boring for anyone.

To ensure that this year we are bringing back the popular games & activities from last year, like …

Assorted Chaat & Refreshments Stalls
Bouncy Houses
Pony Rides & Face Painting along with a few other fun games & activities that are new this year, like …

IALFM SUNDAY SCHOOL ACTIVITIES

Our Sunday School is also organizing different activities which include …

Bike-a-Thon, Cake Walk, Bake Sale, Carnival Games, Gift Basket Auction,
Fruit Kabobs, Chaat Stalls & Tug of War

PLEASE COME AND ATTEND THE FABULOUS PICNIC EVENT PLANNED FOR YOU AND YOUR FAMILY!

We hope to see all of you there!

Let us know if you will have any questions by dropping us an email at picnic@ialfm.org

item.title: IALFM BoD Election (Term: 2019-20)
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Announcement: IALFM Board of Directors Election Results, Term: 2019-20 (12/28/2018)

We are pleased to announce the results of IALFM BoD Elections (Term: 2019-20).The election was held to elect 4 directors for open positions in the IALFM Board of Directors.

IALFM Board of Directors Election Results
Election started on Dec 12, 2018 at 9:00 am CT
Voting ended on Dec 27, 2018 at 11:00 pm CT
Voting Ballots were sent to 90 IALFM Members
65 IALFM Members voted in the elections
Abstentions: 0
Therefore, the turnout was 72%

Candidates & Votes Secured
Br Ahmed Elsaie: 57 votes
Br Ali Emran: 49 votes
Br Gamal Abdel-Hafiz: 47 votes
Br Mahmood Anwar: 35 votes
Br Salman Sattar: 28 votes
Br Sher Khan: 44 votes

Four Elected Candidates
Br Ahmed Elsaie (57 votes)
Br Ali Emran (49 votes)
Br Gamal Abdel-Hafiz (47 votes)
Br Sher Khan (44 votes)

Thanks to all the candidates for their participation in this election. We really appreciate their willingness to serve the community.

We welcome the newly elected directors for IALFM BOD and wish the new board all the best in their endeavors. We also pray to Allah to support & guide them to strengthen our community. Thanks to outgoing directors who worked hard in their respective positions for the benefit of our community.

In the end, the Election Commission also thanks the community for trusting us with this responsibility. Please let us know if you have any questions. You can send us an email at: election@ialfm.org

May Allah bless you & your family!
Jazak Allah Khair!
_____________________________________________
Brs. Aminur Rahman, Mohammad Qasim & Sabir Ali
IALFM Election Commission

====== XXXX ================== XXXX ================== XXXX ======

Announcement: Vote for IALFM Board of Directors Election, Term: 2019-20 (12/12/2018)

IALFM Election Commission has announced that the following 6 brothers have accepted the nomination for IALFM Board of Directors election for 2019-20 term.

1. Br Ahmed Elsaie
2. Br Ali Emran    
3. Br Gamal Abdel-Hafiz
4. Br Mahmood Anwar    
5. Br Salman Sattar    
6. Br Sher Khan

There are 4 open positions on the IALFM BoD and we have 6 candidates. Therefore, an election is being held and all eligible IAFLM members can vote and elect 4 new directors for the IALFM Board.

Election is being held by E-Vote ('Electronic Voting') mechanism using electionbuddy.com platform. The voting process started on Wed Dec 12 and will close on Thursday Dec 27, 2017 (11pm CT). Election results will be announced the following day, on Friday Dec 28, 2017 before noon Insha Allah, & also after Friday prayers at the masjid. All eligible IALFM members should have received an email from IALFM Islamic Association of Lewisville-Flower Mound <invitations@app.electionbuddy.com> at their respective unique email addresses that we have in our records, which has a unique secure link to their website to cast their vote. Submitted ballots will be automatically tallied by Electionbuddy system and results will be available to Election Commission for announcement at the end of voting period.

Note: If you have a 'Family Membership', everyone 18 years or older in your family is eligible to vote in this election. You can request for a ballot to be sent to every eligible family member's email address, if one is not received due to any reason. Please send us their Full Names, unique personal email address & year of birth (in case they are your children, 18 years or over).

Please let us know if you have any questions or need clarification. You can send us an email at: election@ialfm.org

====== XXXX ================== XXXX ================== XXXX ======

Announcement: Nominations are open now (11/12/2018)

We seek your support in electing new directors for four (4) open positions in the IALFM Board of Directors (BoD).

The following four (4) brothers will be leaving the IALFM BoD by the end of this year.

1. Br. Amir Haque (term expiring, eligible for re-election)
2. Br. Mehmud Ali (not able to continue in 2019)
3. Br. Mujtaba Hassan (term expiring, eligible for re-election)
4. Br. Nasir Malik (not able to continue in 2019)

They have been incredible asset to our organization's board. IALFM community is thankful to them for their contributions, commitment and passion.

IALFM is holding election for four (4) available positions in the 'Board of Directors and thru this email, we seek your nominations and support for election process.

Nomination Process
As IALFM members, you are hereby requested to provide nominations for candidates.

If you need to verify your or your nominee's membership status, a current ‘Members List’ is available here (click to open the PDF document) & its also posted on the Notice Board in Masjid (inside men's entrance area). You need to be a member of IALFM for at least six (6) months to be eligible to vote (as per Articles 3.1.2 & 3.1.3, read together in our constitution).

The nomination form is available on our website over here (click to open the PDF document). You can nominate any number of candidates. Please download & return the completed form (with your nominations) in person to an Election Commission member (see names below), or electronically via email to the Election Commission at election@ialfm.org

In order to complete the election process in time, please submit your nomination(s) no later than November 30, 2018. Any nomination received after this date will not be considered for elections.

Following that, the Election Commission will collect the nominations, validate eligibility, and start voting process, if needed.

Please note, eligible candidates for the BOD Elections must be IALFM members in good standing for at least one (1) year (as per Article 7.1.2 in our constitution). And you cannot nominate members of Election Commission for open BoD positions (our names are noted below).

If you have any questions or need clarification, please feel free to contact Election Commission via email at election@ialfm.org. We need your full support in this process!

May Allah bless you & your family!
Jazak Allah Khair!
_______________________________________________
Brs. Aminur Rahman, Mohammad Qasim & Sabir Ali
IALFM Election Commission

====== XXXX ================== XXXX ================== XXXX ======

Announcement: New Election Commission (11/7/2018)

Please note that as a result of email outreach after last General Body Meeting, three (3) brothers from IALFM Community have volunteered to serve on the Election Commission. They will be working together to hold the upcoming election for IALFM Board of Directors.

They are Br. Aminur Rahman, Dr Mohammad Qasim & Br Sabir Ali.

It is important for us as a community that we lend our support to these commission members, so that they can successfully hold election for open positions in IALFM Board of Directors.

Election Commission will soon be sharing more details on the election process & requesting nominations for open positions. They can be reached via email at election@ialfm.org

Sincerely,
IALFM Board of Directors
bod@ialfm.org

-------------------------------------------------------------------

Signup for IALFM Membership

item.title: Instructions for 'IALFM Live' Meeting
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Assalam-o-Alaikum - In order to attend the 'IALFM Live' meeting that has been scheduled by IALFM, please follow these instructions:

  1. Install Microsoft Teams app on your computer or mobile device of any type (i.e. Windows, Mac, Apple iOS, Google Android) by going to this website: https://teams.microsoft.com/downloads (installing Teams app on your computer or mobile device is highly recommended for best experience)

    Note: If you are planning to join from a computer (Windows or Mac), you can join the meeting using an internet browser as well, such as Google Chrome or Microsoft Edge (fully supported browsers). Just click on the 'Join on the web instead' button in the browser window, after Step 3 below. However, on mobile devices, you will have to install Teams app to join this meeting.
  2. After the app installation completes, there is no need for you to sign up for a free account or sign in using any type of account that you may already have
  3. At the time of the meeting or a few minutes before it, i.e. 7pm CT, click on this link: Join Microsoft Teams Meeting
  4. When the Microsoft Teams app comes up (after you clicked on the above link to join the meeting), click on 'Join as a guest' option to join the meeting (again, no need to sign in)
  5. On the next screen, provide your full name (like, Ahmad Khan) and then click on 'Join meeting' button to join the meeting
  6. After this, you'll just end up in the meeting with the organizer and other attendees like you
  7. Happy meeting!

    For Technical Issues: Please send an email to live@ialfm.org

item.title: 'IALFM Live' community meeting bi-weekly on Sundays at 7pm
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Board has scheduled a bi-weekly community meeting to keep in touch with our community members, where updates are provided related to different activities at the masjid. Since starting this practice back in March 2020, we have shared updates on:

  1. Construction Committee's tireless efforts for our New Masjid Building Construction Project
  2. Ramadan Committee's efforts to distribute boxed meals to our community members & first responders at FMPD & FMFD and Presbyterian Hospital
  3. ERF Committee's work to provide emergency financial relief to Muslim community members at large in DFW metropolitan area during these difficult times of us going thru a pandemic
  4. Fundraising Committee's efforts to raise $300K during Ramadan Virtual Construction Fundraiser

Please join us every other week on Sunday at 7pm for a sync up on IALFM Masjid and community affairs! See online meeting details below.

Zoom Video Meeting Details:
Join Zoom Meeting
(https://zoom.us/j/9705912621)
Meeting ID: 970 591 2621
One Tap Mobile: +13462487799,,9705912621# US
or +12532158782,,9705912621# US
(Find your local number: https://zoom.us/u/acl9Gqlobp)

For Technical Issues: Please send an email to live@ialfm.org

item.title: Ramadan Community Iftar @ IALFM
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Ramadan Community Iftar
As per IALFM tradition, we are planning to organize Community Iftar every week on Friday, Saturday & Sunday during the month of Ramadan. We will be hosting community iftar in person at the masjid premises, as we have done in the past, with appropriate safety protocols. We are counting on IALFM community and families to sign-up for available slots in the sign-up sheet linked below. Ramadan Committee has recommended to have 8 or more families per day to sponsor community iftar on a given day. Please read guidelines provided in the sign-up sheet linked below.

Community Iftar Sign-up (any questions/feedback: ramadan@ialfm.org)

Ramadan Iftar

If you are signing up to for individual Iftar sponsor slots, please use the following link to donate for 'Ramadan Iftar'. Individuals can donate any amount that they desire to donate for this purpose. We will use it to arrange for Iftar on days that are not sponsored by family groups, like 27th Ramadan, Interfaith Iftar, or any other remaining days.

Donate for Ramadan Iftar

Ramadan Programs

Community Members are also requested to donate for the Ramadan Programs (Huffaz for Tarawih, etc.) and other additional expenses (utilities, etc.) that we incur during Ramadan. As in the past, we are requesting all families to donate at least $200 in this regard.

Donate for Ramadan Programs/Expenses

Be a Ramadan Volunteer!

Please sign up to be a Volunteer during Ramadan to earn maximum rewards from Allah!

Ramadan Volunter Sign-up Sheet

Donate for Ramadan Food Drive (for 200 Food Boxes for local Refugee families, cost is $75 per food box)

Ramadan 2026 Announcement from FCNA (IALFM follows FCNA)

Ramadan Calendar 2026

IALFM Link-tree

Want to be informed on all IALFM activities?
Please sign-up for our 'Weekly Newsletter' (on our main website/landing page) & follow our 'IALFM Masjid' page on Facebook for all Ramadan programs & activities!

May Allah bestow the blessings of the month of Ramadan on all of us, Aameen!

Any questions or feedback, please send it to: bod@ialfm.org

item.title: IALFM: A Message of Solidarity!
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Given the recent tragic events and divisive state of our country at the moment, it is critical for us to come together as an Ummah and stand by our Brothers and Sisters.

The Islamic Association of Lewisville & Flower Mound (IALFM) expresses the deepest concern regarding the current unrest within America and conveys condolences to all, who have lost their loved ones at the hands of the systemic racism that exists in our society. We must work together to construct a healthier, accepting community for all regardless of race and ethnicity, starting within our own community. We understand that these changes will be challenging, but we have been taught by our beloved Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) to always side with the right and just. We strive to create and foster an environment that not simply accepts but embraces all, without any discrimination.

We pray to God that He guides us out of this dark time and allows us to grow into a beacon of acceptance, understanding and peace. Aameen!

- IALFM Board of Directors on behalf of community
bod@ialfm.org

item.title: IALFM Quarterly General Body Meeting on Sun. June 14 @ 7pm
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM General Body Meeting has been scheduled for Sunday, June 14, 2020 @ 7pm via Zoom (virtual online meeting, more details will be shared here soon).

IALFM community members are encouraged to attend this meeting to review the Masjid activities during the first half of 2020, provide feedback and pose questions to IALFM Board & Committee members.

Agenda:
7p - Opening Dua by Imam Rasheed
7:05p - Opening statement by President (Br Gamal Abdel-Hafiz)
7:15p - Masjid Construction update by President
7:20p - Financial & Fundraising Status by Treasurer (Br Amir Ehtisham)
7:30p - Ramadan 2020 Activities (Br Ali Khokhar)
7:35p - Committees signup & updates by Secretary (Br Salman Sattar)
7:40p - Youth & Educational activities (Sr Maha Ali)
7:50p - Q&A Session
8:10p - Closing Dua by Imam

Please plan on attending and keep yourself updated on IALFM affairs!
Questions: bod@ialfm.org

item.title: Start Here | Connect with IALFM Masjid!
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
In our new IALFM Masjid building, our congregation offers five daily prayers, Friday prayers, Ramadan and Eid prayers. We also operate a Quran School, Sunday School & Pillars Academy for our kids and hold other events for youth & sisters. The multipurpose hall (aka MPH) is also used for sports and other special community or family events and activities.

CONNECT WITH IALFM MASJID

  • Subscribe to our Weekly Newsletter, which is sent via email on Thursdays
  • Follow IALFM Masjid page on Facebook to get timely updates on all events around the year, including educational (for all age groups) & youth events
  • Join IALFM Announcements WhatsApp group to receive timely announcements and updates throughout the year. You can do that at the masjid or send an email to our Board asking for a link to join
  • Check out IALFM Link-tree page for quick links to popular asks or frequently requested resources
  • If you meet the IALFM's membership eligibility requirements, please sign up for individual or family membership and have your say in how IALFM is governed & operated
  • Check out IALFM Youth Link-tree page for details on upcoming events for youth, their Instagram & Facebook pages, Snapchat & multiple WhatsApp groups

Let us know via email if you have any questions or concerns. We welcome your feedback!

Board of Directors @ IALFM

item.title: Quran School | Student Registration
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
Students from all levels (beginners to advance) are welcome to join!

Students can opt for Online or Onsite (at the masjid) class & lesson delivery option.

Our Quran School provides Quran classes with emphasis on Tajweed for all levels. Tajweed refers to the rules governing the pronunciation aspects during recitation of Quran, just the way it was recited in the times of our Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him). We provide Quran Tajweed classes for beginners to advance level students.

Quran Teachers:

Imam Rasheed Farah
Sr. Asma Qureshi
Sr. Yasmeen Almasri

Monthly Tuition Fee:

$75 for each student
Note: Due to increased costs associated with the hiring of well-qualified Quran Tajweed teachers, we do not offer any discounts for multiple kids or students belonging to the same family.

Pay Fees Online:

You can pay Quran School Fees online by clicking here!

For Program Details & Registration, please go to our Quran Nazira Program page ...

Student Registration Form

If you have any questions, please contact Sr Samia Babar via email at quranschool@ialfm.org

item.title: Hiring Education/Youth Director & Khateeb for IALFM
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM is currently looking to hire an Education/Youth Director & Khateeb.

Interested candidates can review the job description below.

Qualified candidates can apply for the position by sending their cover letter & resume to: careers@ialfm.org

Job Description

Objective
IALFM seeks an Education/Youth Director and Khateeb to organize and run education and youth related activities for the Muslim community of IALFM. The Education Director should provide education to our community based on the Holy Quran and Sunnah of the Prophet (SAW). The Director will work with the Board and relevant committees for the coordination and planning of all programs.

Profile
The ideal candidate will be someone who:

  • has been raised in the US
  • has first-hand experience of public education system
  • understands issues of Muslim communities in US
  • has solid Islamic education and training
  • is associated with or a graduate of a reputable Islamic institute
  • has experience of working with both youth and adults
  • has experience with providing counseling
  • is articulate and has good communication skills
  • can connect with and engage people
  • has experience of interfaith activities and can organize open house and represent our community at local events
  • has experience with fundraising
  • resides close to the community and is perceived to be part of the community and not as an outsider
  • is available over evenings and weekends

Responsibilities

a) Plan and organize calendar of events
b) Jummah Khutbahs, as needed
c) Educational Services
        - Classes on a regular basis
        - Friday night Halaqas / Family nights
        - Sisters educational classes
d) Youth activities
        - Interactive Halaqas that engage participants
        - Experiential learning through sports, games, outings and other fun activities
        - Counseling
e) Advises on Sunday School Curriculum
f) Use digital channels to engage the community and reinforce learning
g) Support Outreach Programs (Dawah, Interfaith dialogue). Help with Masjid open house and serve as a speaker and representative for interfaith programs
h) Speak at Fundraising Events of Islamic Association or to represent Association at events
i) Ramadan programs (daily khatirah etc.)
j) Working with the BOD and Imam to solve community problems
k) Measure event performance and provide reports on effectiveness of programs

item.title: Donate to ICNA Relief Campaign
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
We humbly request our community members, other Muslims and humanity at large to generously donate to this campaign from ICNA Relief ...

item.title: BoD Nominations
item.dc:creator: babar
item.content:encoded:
Nominations are open for 3 BoD positions till November 21. Please see details here and below. Send nominations to election@ialfm.org

Are you interested in making a difference by joining the board? 
Do you know a member of IALFM who you think is a good candidate for the board? ​​Follow the guidelines below to provide nominations for candidates.

1. Eligible candidates should be registered members of IALFM as per the list linked below. A member can also nominate him or herself (self-nomination is allowed).

2. If you need to verify your or your nominee's membership status, a current ‘Members List’ is available here (click to open the PDF document).​ This list is maintained by Membership team, detailed information is included below.​

3. The easiest way to show your interest​ to serve​ or to nominate someone​ (up to 4 members)​ is to reply to this email (or send email to election@ialfm.org). Share the name​​ and email of ​each​ nominee. If you prefer a paper version, the nomination form is also available over here (click to open the PDF document). Please download, fill & return the completed form (with your nominations) electronically via email to the Election Commission at election@ialfm.org​

4. In order to complete the election process in time, please submit your nomination(s) no later than November 21, 2022. Any nomination received after this date will not be considered for elections.

​5. The Election Commission will validate eligibility​ of those nominated and then​ verify ​interest and availability of the​ nominees. If more than three eligible nominees are willing to serve, then Election Commission will  ​proceed with the ​electronic ​voting process.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Nominations for elections for 3 BoD seats are open till Nov 21. Please see details here on how to nominate yourself or someone. Send nominations to election@ialfm.org

item.title: Educational & Youth Programs @ IALFM
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
IALFM Educational events and Youth programs are advertised thru our Weekly Newsletter, Community Announcement WhatsApp group and on our Facebook page. Please subscribe to those channels for more information and timely updates. See relevant info listed below. Looking forward to have you join our events & activities with others in the IALFM Community!

FOR IALFM EDUCATIONAL EVENTS & YOUTH PROGRAMS INFO

Subscribe to our Weekly Newsletter, which is sent via email to everyone in our community a day or two before Friday.

Join IALFM Announcements WhatsApp group to receive timely announcements and updates thru out the year.

Follow IALFM Masjid page on Facebook to get timely updates on all events around the year, including educational (for all age groups) & youth events.

Check out IALFM Youth Link-tree page for details on upcoming events for youth, their Instagram & Facebook pages, Snapchat & multiple WhatsApp groups.

item.title: Open Position @ IALFM: Sunday School Principal
item.dc:creator: ialfm_v5vile
item.content:encoded:
We're looking to hire a Sunday School Principal for the 2024-2025 academic year and Summer Camp in 2025. To apply, please send your resume to bod@ialfm.org

IALFM Sunday School Principal's role is to lead and manage Sunday School with the assistance of the Sunday School Committee and the team of volunteers. This part-time, paid position entails responsibilities such as operating Sunday School, administering curriculum, leading the volunteer team, managing technical resources and supplies, communicating with parents, addressing any student discipline issues, and leading Summer Camp. The Principal is also responsible for organizing Sunday School events and addressing ad-hoc issues.

Detailed Requirements for the Sunday School Principal’s role:

1. Lead 'Sunday School Committee' and operate Sunday School with committee’s assistance.

2. Schedule and facilitate 'Sunday School Committee’ meetings to plan and execute the responsibilities of Sunday school.

3. Organize 'Registration Day' and related activities (leverage volunteers).

4. Process tuition fee and other charges in MOHID, the masjid financial system.

5. Manage curriculum and syllabus for all five levels of Sunday school.

6. Plan classroom setup, track and optimize student enrollment.

7. Lead and organize registration process for existing and new volunteers for all roles, and conduct volunteer training before start of school year.

8. Plan and finalize volunteers' assignment for all levels, make changes as necessary, ensure volunteer/teacher availability at all times to conduct classes (take care of class setup every Sunday with help from volunteers).

9.   Publish and update Sunday School related information on IALFM website.

10. Manage and maintain all technical resources in the Sunday School office/masjid (laptops, printers, internet access devices, etc.).

11. Order and keep inventory of all supplies.

12. Organize and lead Sunday School events.

13. Communicate with parents via email and chat (Mailchimp and WhatsApp).

14. Partner with volunteers to design lesson plans, track attendance, test results and other related records.

15.  Address student discipline issues by working with teachers, committee and parents.

16.  Address ad-hoc issues related to Masjid premises (classrooms, playground, general premises maintenance, etc.).

17.  Lead and organize Summer Camp.

How to apply: To apply, please send your resume to bod@ialfm.org

  • Free and open to Public: RSVP here

  • Refreshments will be served

item.title: Full‑Time Male & Female Hifz Instructors & Program Leads
item.dc:creator: secretary
item.content:encoded:
Full‑Time Male & Female Hifz Instructors & Program Leads

IALFM – Hifz & Tarbiyah Program
Anticipated Start Date: June 2026 (TBD)

Position Overview
IALFM is seeking committed, qualified full‑time Male and Female Hifz Instructors and Program Leads to join its Hifz & Tarbiyah Program. The role combines structured Qur’an memorization with intentional Islamic character development, discipline, and spiritual growth in a nurturing, focused environment.

Primary Responsibilities

Hifz Instruction & Student Development

  • Lead daily Hifz instruction including new memorization, revision, and long‑term murājaʿah
  • Ensure strong retention, fluency, accuracy, and correct Tajwīd
  • Monitor progress, identify learning gaps, and adapt instruction as needed
  • Maintain a disciplined, focused, and positive classroom environment

Tarbiyah & Islamic Studies

  • Implement Tarbiyah curriculum with emphasis on akhlaq, discipline, and responsibility
  • Teach foundational Islamic studies appropriate for Tahfiz students, including Seerah, adab, and basic fiqh
  • Model and reinforce Islamic manners within the classroom and masjid

Leadership, Mentorship & Collaboration

  • Mentor teachers, assistants, and approved volunteers
  • Support curriculum development, teaching methodologies, and student outcomes
  • Participate in assessments, placements, and program events
  • Maintain professional communication with parents and administration
  • Participate in a 360‑degree feedback and continuous improvement process

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Completed Hifz with strong retention and sound Tajwīd
  • Strong mastery of theoretical and applied Tajwīd
  • Experience teaching Hifz or working in structured educational settings
  • Excellent classroom management and organizational skills
  • Patient, compassionate, and motivating teaching style
  • Ability to communicate clearly with parents and leadership
  • Commitment to long‑term professional growth

Work Schedule & Reporting

Full‑time, weekday schedule aligned with the Tahfiz program calendar. Occasional evenings or weekends may be required for special programs or events.

This position reports to the Religious Director and Board of Directors.

Salary Range & Benefits

  • Salary Range: $50,000 – $65,000 annually
  • Compensation based on experience and ability to teach young children
  • Benefits include medical coverage and paid time off

References

3–5 relevant professional references are required.

How to Apply
Interested candidates should email their résumé and references to:
hifz@ialfm.org
